papers.title: Discovering Physical Directions in Weight Space: Composing Neural PDE Experts
papers.summary: Recent advances in neural operators have made partial differential equation (PDE) surrogate modeling increasingly scalable and transferable through large-scale pretraining and in-context adaptation. However, after a shared operator is fine-tuned to multiple regimes within a continuous physical family, it remains unclear whether the resulting weight-space updates merely form isolated regime experts or reveal reusable physical structure. Starting from a shared family anchor, we fine-tune low- and high-regime endpoint experts and show that their updates can be separated into a family-shared adaptation and a direction aligned with the underlying physical parameter. This separation reinterprets endpoint experts as finite-difference probes of a local physical direction in weight space, explaining why static averaging can interpolate between regimes but attenuates endpoint-specific physics. Building on this perspective, we propose Calibration-Conditioned Merge (CCM), a post-hoc coordinate readout method for composing neural PDE experts along this physical direction. Given physical metadata, a calibrated coordinate mapping, or a short observed rollout prefix, CCM infers the target composition coordinate and deploys a single merged checkpoint for the remaining rollout. We evaluate CCM on the reaction--diffusion system, viscosity-parameterized two-dimensional Navier--Stokes equations, and radial dam-break dynamics. Across these benchmarks, CCM achieves its strongest gains in extrapolative regimes, reducing out-of-distribution rollout error relative to the family anchor by 54.2%, 42.8%, and 13.8%, respectively. Further experiments across FNO scales, a DPOT-style backbone, and ablations confirm that endpoint fine-tuning is not arbitrary checkpoint drift, but reveals a calibratable physical direction for training-free transfer across PDE regimes.

ai.tldr: 提出在权重空间中沿物理方向组合神经PDE专家模型的方法，无需额外训练即可跨物理参数迁移。
ai.motivation: 现有神经算子微调后得到多个物理区域的专家模型，但权重更新是否蕴含可复用的物理结构尚不明确。
ai.method: 通过微调低/高物理参数端点专家，分离出族共享适应和沿物理参数的方向；提出校准条件合并（CCM），利用物理元数据或短时观测推断目标组合坐标，生成单一合并检查点。
ai.result: 在反应扩散、二维Navier-Stokes和径向溃坝动力学上，CCM在推演区域将分布外误差分别降低54.2%、42.8%和13.8%。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何利用权重空间中的物理方向实现零训练跨参数迁移，提升PDE代理模型的泛化能力。

idea_spark.idea: 将专家模型视为物理方向的有限差分探针，通过坐标映射实现连续物理参数空间的插值。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖端点专家微调的质量和物理参数方向的线性假设，非线性强耦合系统可能失效。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受有限差分法启发，将离散专家模型组合转化为连续物理方向的数值近似。

en.tldr: Proposes a method to compose neural PDE experts along physical directions in weight space, enabling training-free transfer across PDE regimes.
en.motivation: After fine-tuning a shared neural operator to multiple regimes, it is unclear whether weight updates reveal reusable physical structure or are just isolated experts.
en.method: Fine-tune low- and high-regime endpoint experts to separate family-shared adaptation from a direction aligned with the physical parameter; propose Calibration-Conditioned Merge (CCM) to infer target composition coordinate from physical metadata or short rollout prefix and deploy a single merged checkpoint.
en.result: On reaction-diffusion, 2D Navier-Stokes, and radial dam-break, CCM reduces out-of-distribution rollout error by 54.2%, 42.8%, and 13.8% respectively.
en.help_to_user: Helps users leverage physical directions in weight space for zero-training cross-parameter transfer, improving PDE surrogate generalization.

idea_spark.idea: Treat expert models as finite-difference probes of a physical direction, enabling interpolation in continuous parameter space via coordinate mapping.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on quality of endpoint fine-tuning and linearity assumption of physical direction; may fail for strongly nonlinear coupled systems.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by finite-difference methods, discretizing expert models into numerical approximations of continuous physical directions.

papers.title: PI-SONet: A Physics-Informed Symplectic Operator Network for Real-Time Optimal Control of Multi-Agent Systems
papers.summary: Many real-life applications involve controlling high-dimensional multi-agent systems in real-time. Existing optimal control solvers often suffer from the curse-of-dimensionality and require complete rerunning for each new problem setting. We target nonconvex, nonlinear problems in 100s of dimensions by introducing PI-SONet (Physics-Informed Symplectic Operator Network), a structure-preserving operator learning framework for solving parameterized families of optimal control problems and their Pontraygin Maximum Principle (PMP) systems. PI-SONet combines a latent right-space solver with a conditional symplectic operator to produce tractable Hamiltonian trajectories in a computationally efficient auxiliary space and transform them back to physical space. This decomposition yields a \textit{single} trained operator that approximates the PMP solution map, inherently preserves Hamiltonian structure, and generalizes across unseen problem configurations. Unlike existing methods, which are fundamentally single-instance solvers, PI-SONet achieves sub-second inferences on new problem instances, equating to up to 10,000x speedup over representative baselines. These results suggest that structure-preserving neural operators provide a practical route toward reusable, real-time surrogates for high-dimensional optimal control.

ai.tldr: 提出一种保结构的算子学习框架PI-SONet，用于实时求解高维多智能体系统的最优控制问题，速度比传统方法快上万倍。
ai.motivation: 现有最优控制求解器受维数灾难困扰，且每个新问题需完全重新计算，无法满足实时控制需求。
ai.method: 将潜空间求解器与条件辛算子结合，在计算高效的辅助空间中生成哈密顿轨迹，再变换回物理空间，从而学习参数化最优控制问题的PMP解映射。
ai.result: 单个训练好的算子可泛化到未见问题配置，推理时间亚秒级，相比基线加速高达10,000倍。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户快速获得高维非线性最优控制问题的近似解，适用于需要实时响应的多智能体场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将辛结构约束引入神经算子，使模型在低维潜空间高效计算哈密顿轨迹，再解码回物理空间。
idea_spark.risk: 潜空间维度和变换的选取可能影响精度；对非哈密顿系统需额外处理。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受辛积分器与物理信息神经网络启发，将结构保持与算子学习结合，实现可复用的实时代理模型。

en.tldr: Proposes PI-SONet, a structure-preserving operator learning framework for real-time optimal control of high-dimensional multi-agent systems, achieving up to 10,000x speedup over baselines.
en.motivation: Existing optimal control solvers suffer from the curse of dimensionality and require complete rerunning for each new problem, hindering real-time applications.
en.method: Combines a latent right-space solver with a conditional symplectic operator to generate tractable Hamiltonian trajectories in an auxiliary space and transform them back to physical space, learning the PMP solution map.
en.result: A single trained operator generalizes across unseen problem configurations, delivering sub-second inferences with up to 10,000x speedup over representative baselines.
en.help_to_user: Enables users to obtain approximate solutions to high-dimensional nonlinear optimal control problems in real time, suitable for multi-agent systems requiring rapid responses.

idea_spark.idea: Incorporate symplectic structure constraints into neural operators to efficiently compute Hamiltonian trajectories in a low-dimensional latent space and decode them back to physical space.
idea_spark.risk: Choice of latent space dimension and transformation may affect accuracy; additional handling is needed for non-Hamiltonian systems.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by symplectic integrators and physics-informed neural networks, combining structure preservation with operator learning to create reusable real-time surrogates.

papers.title: Scalable Mechanistic Neural Networks for Differential Equations and Machine Learning
papers.summary: We propose Scalable Mechanistic Neural Network (S-MNN), an enhanced neural network framework designed for scientific machine learning applications involving long temporal sequences. By reformulating the original Mechanistic Neural Network (MNN) (Pervez et al., 2024), we reduce the computational time and space complexities from cubic and quadratic with respect to the sequence length, respectively, to linear. This significant improvement enables efficient modeling of long-term dynamics without sacrificing accuracy or interpretability. Extensive experiments demonstrate that S-MNN matches the original MNN in precision while substantially reducing computational resources. Consequently, S-MNN can drop-in replace the original MNN in applications, providing a practical and efficient tool for integrating mechanistic bottlenecks into neural network models of complex dynamical systems. Source code is available at https://github.com/IST-DASLab/ScalableMNN.

ai.tldr: 提出可扩展机制神经网络（S-MNN），将序列长度相关的计算复杂度从三次/二次降至线性，实现高效长期动力学建模。
ai.motivation: 原始机制神经网络（MNN）在处理长时序时计算复杂度高，限制了其在科学机器学习中的应用。
ai.method: 通过重新公式化MNN，将时间和空间复杂度分别从三次和二次降至线性，同时保持精度和可解释性。
ai.result: S-MNN在精度上与原始MNN相当，但计算资源大幅减少，可即插即用替代MNN。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种高效、可扩展的神经网络框架，适用于需要长期动力学建模的科学计算任务。

idea_spark.idea: 将机制瓶颈与线性复杂度结合，可能启发新型高效物理信息神经网络。
idea_spark.risk: 线性近似可能在某些极端长序列或高非线性系统中引入精度损失。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受MNN和序列模型计算优化启发，探索机制建模与计算效率的平衡。

en.tldr: Proposes Scalable Mechanistic Neural Network (S-MNN), reducing computational complexity from cubic/quadratic to linear in sequence length for efficient long-term dynamics modeling.
en.motivation: Original Mechanistic Neural Network (MNN) suffers from high computational cost for long sequences, limiting its use in scientific machine learning.
en.method: Reformulates MNN to reduce time and space complexities from cubic and quadratic to linear, while preserving accuracy and interpretability.
en.result: S-MNN matches original MNN precision with significantly lower resource usage, enabling drop-in replacement.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient, scalable neural network framework for scientific computing tasks requiring long-term dynamics modeling.

idea_spark.idea: Combining mechanistic bottlenecks with linear complexity may inspire new efficient physics-informed neural networks.
idea_spark.risk: Linear approximations may introduce accuracy loss in extremely long sequences or highly nonlinear systems.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by MNN and sequence model optimization, exploring the balance between mechanistic modeling and computational efficiency.

papers.title: Scalable Solution of the Stochastic Multi-path Traveling Salesman Problem via Neural Networks
papers.summary: The multi-path Traveling Salesman Problem with stochastic travel costs arises in hybrid vehicle routing applications designed for Smart City and City Logistics, where multiple paths exist between each pair of locations. Travel times along these paths are typically affected by real-time traffic conditions and therefore modeled as stochastic. The objective of the problem is to determine a Hamiltonian tour that minimizes the expected total travel cost under uncertainty. In this work, we adopt a two-stage stochastic programming formulation. In the first stage, a predefined route specifying the sequence of locations to be visited is determined, while taking into consideration a second-stage recourse problem that selects the optimal path from the feasible set of alternative paths for each pair of locations, once real-time traffic conditions are realized. To reduce the computational burden imposed by the large number of scenarios required to capture travel time uncertainty, the innovation of this work is the integration of neural network-based surrogate models to approximate the expected value of the second-stage recourse problem. Different architectures and training strategies for the neural networks are proposed and analyzed, with performance evaluated in terms of computation time, solution quality, and generalization capability. Preliminary findings demonstrate the enhanced scalability and practical applicability of the approach for complex vehicle routing problems under uncertainty.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于神经网络代理模型的可扩展方法，用于求解随机多路径旅行商问题，以降低两阶段随机规划中大量场景带来的计算负担。
ai.motivation: 智能城市和城市物流中的混合动力车辆路径问题涉及随机旅行成本，传统方法因场景数量庞大而计算困难。
ai.method: 采用两阶段随机规划，第一阶段确定访问顺序，第二阶段在实时交通信息实现后选择最优路径；用神经网络代理模型近似第二阶段期望值。
ai.result: 初步结果表明该方法在计算时间、解质量和泛化能力上具有更好的可扩展性和实际适用性。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户高效求解复杂随机车辆路径问题，减少计算资源消耗。

idea_spark.idea: 将神经网络代理模型与强化学习结合，在线自适应调整路径选择策略。
idea_spark.risk: 代理模型可能引入近似误差，在极端交通场景下泛化能力不足。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受深度神经网络在函数逼近中成功应用的启发，将其用于随机规划中的期望值近似。

en.tldr: Proposes a scalable neural network surrogate approach for the stochastic multi-path traveling salesman problem, reducing computational burden from large scenario sets in two-stage stochastic programming.
en.motivation: Hybrid vehicle routing in smart city logistics involves stochastic travel costs; traditional methods struggle with high scenario counts.
en.method: Two-stage stochastic programming: first stage decides visit sequence, second stage selects optimal path after real-time traffic realization; neural network surrogates approximate the expected second-stage cost.
en.result: Preliminary results show improved scalability, solution quality, and generalization for complex uncertain vehicle routing problems.
en.help_to_user: Enables efficient solution of complex stochastic vehicle routing problems with reduced computational resources.

idea_spark.idea: Combine neural network surrogates with reinforcement learning for online adaptive path selection.
idea_spark.risk: Surrogate approximation errors may degrade performance under extreme traffic scenarios.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the success of deep neural networks in function approximation, applied here to approximate expected recourse costs in stochastic programming.

papers.title: Revealing dynamics of non-autonomous complex systems from data
papers.summary: Discovering governing equations from data is crucial for understanding complex systems in many diverse fields from science to engineering. Yet, there still is a lack of versatile computational toolbox to deal with this long standing challenge due to the inherent non-autonomicity and unknowability of the underlying dynamics. Here, we introduce a data-driven approach for inferring non-autonomous dynamical equations by identifying an optimal set of basis functions within the model space, enabling the reconstruction of complex systems behavior under simplified prior specifications. Our method demonstrates effectiveness in equation discovery on canonical synthetic systems such as cusp bifurcation and coupled Kuramoto oscillators. Furthermore, we extend the application of this approach to leaf cellular energy, unmanned aerial vehicle navigation, chick-heart aggregates, and marine fish community under simple basis function libraries. Leveraging the inferred equations, we accurately predict the evolution of these empirical systems and further uncover their governing laws. Our approach offers a novel paradigm to reveal the underlying dynamics of a wide range of real-world systems.

ai.tldr: 提出一种数据驱动方法，通过识别最优基函数来推断非自治动力方程，适用于多种真实世界系统。
ai.motivation: 现有方法难以处理非自治和未知动力学的复杂系统，缺乏通用计算工具。
ai.method: 在模型空间内识别最优基函数集，以简化先验假设重建系统行为。
ai.result: 在尖点分岔、耦合Kuramoto振子等合成系统及叶细胞能量、无人机导航、鸡心聚集体、海洋鱼类群落等实证系统中成功发现方程并预测演化。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种从数据中自动发现非自治系统控制方程的新范式，降低对先验知识的依赖。

idea_spark.idea: 结合稀疏回归与基函数库自适应选择，可进一步扩展到高维或噪声数据。
idea_spark.risk: 基函数库的选择可能影响泛化能力，对高度非线性或混沌系统需谨慎验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受稀疏识别非线性动力学（SINDy）启发，但针对非自治性进行了扩展。

en.tldr: A data-driven method that infers non-autonomous dynamical equations by identifying optimal basis functions, demonstrated on synthetic and real-world systems.
en.motivation: Existing methods struggle with non-autonomous and unknown dynamics in complex systems, lacking a versatile computational toolbox.
en.method: Identify an optimal set of basis functions within the model space to reconstruct system behavior under simplified prior specifications.
en.result: Successfully discovered equations and predicted evolution for synthetic systems (cusp bifurcation, coupled Kuramoto oscillators) and empirical systems (leaf cellular energy, UAV navigation, chick-heart aggregates, marine fish community).
en.help_to_user: Provides a new paradigm for automatically discovering governing equations of non-autonomous systems from data, reducing reliance on prior knowledge.

idea_spark.idea: Combining sparse regression with adaptive basis library selection could extend to high-dimensional or noisy data.
idea_spark.risk: Choice of basis library may affect generalization; careful validation is needed for highly nonlinear or chaotic systems.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by sparse identification of nonlinear dynamics (SINDy) but extended to handle non-autonomous dynamics.

papers.title: Eradicating Negative Transfer in Multi-Physics Foundation Models via Sparse Mixture-of-Experts Routing
papers.summary: Scaling Scientific Machine Learning (SciML) toward universal foundation models is bottlenecked by negative transfer: the simultaneous co-training of disparate partial differential equation (PDE) regimes can induce gradient conflict, unstable optimization, and plasticity loss in dense neural operators. In particular, broadband open-channel fluid dynamics and boundary-dominated porous media flows impose incompatible spectral and geometric demands on a single dense parameter path. We introduce Shodh-MoE, a sparse-activated latent transformer architecture for multi-physics transport. Shodh-MoE operates on compressed 16^3 physical latents produced by a physics-informed autoencoder with an intra-tokenizer Helmholtz-style velocity parameterization, restricting decoded states to divergence-free velocity manifolds. The model guarantees exact mass conservation, achieving a physically verifiable velocity divergence of ~2.8 x 10^-10 (evaluated post-hoc in FP64) on 128^3 grids. A Top-1 soft-semantic router dynamically assigns localized latent patches to expert subnetworks, enabling specialized parameter paths for distinct physical mechanisms while preserving shared experts for universal symmetries. In a 20,000-step distributed pretraining run over mixed three-dimensional physical tensors, routing telemetry shows autonomous domain bifurcation: held-out validation tokens from the open-channel domain route exclusively to Expert 0, while porous-media tokens route exclusively to Expert 1. The model converges simultaneously across both regimes, achieving latent validation MSEs of 2.46 x 10^-5 and 9.76 x 10^-6, and decoded physical MSEs of 2.48 x 10^-6 and 1.76 x 10^-6. These results support sparse expert routing as a practical architectural mechanism for mitigating multi-physics interference in universal neural operators.

ai.tldr: 提出Shodh-MoE稀疏专家路由架构，解决多物理场基础模型中的负迁移问题，实现不同PDE机制的同时高效训练。
ai.motivation: 多物理场基础模型因不同PDE机制间的梯度冲突、优化不稳定和塑性损失而受限于负迁移，现有密集神经算子难以同时处理宽频开渠流体和边界主导多孔介质流。
ai.method: 采用物理信息自编码器压缩至16^3潜变量，结合Helmholtz速度参数化保证散度自由；Top-1软语义路由器将局部潜块分配给专家子网络，实现专用参数路径与共享专家并存。
ai.result: 20,000步分布式预训练后，路由自动分域：开渠验证令牌仅路由至专家0，多孔介质令牌仅路由至专家1；潜变量验证MSE分别为2.46e-5和9.76e-6，解码物理MSE分别为2.48e-6和1.76e-6，速度散度约2.8e-10。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种可扩展的稀疏专家路由机制，用于构建通用多物理场神经算子，缓解负迁移并保证物理守恒。

idea_spark.idea: 将物理约束（如散度自由）直接嵌入潜变量表示，结合动态路由，可推广至任意守恒律PDE系统。
idea_spark.risk: 专家路由可能引入负载不均衡或过拟合；物理约束的嵌入需针对不同PDE定制，通用性有限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受自然语言处理中稀疏MoE启发，将其与物理信息编码结合，实现多物理场自动分域训练。

en.tldr: Proposes Shodh-MoE, a sparse mixture-of-experts routing architecture to eradicate negative transfer in multi-physics foundation models, enabling simultaneous training of disparate PDE regimes.
en.motivation: Scaling scientific ML to universal foundation models is bottlenecked by negative transfer from gradient conflicts, unstable optimization, and plasticity loss in dense neural operators when co-training different PDE regimes like open-channel fluid dynamics and porous media flows.
en.method: Uses a physics-informed autoencoder to compress to 16^3 latent variables with Helmholtz-style velocity parameterization ensuring divergence-free manifolds; a Top-1 soft-semantic router assigns localized latent patches to expert subnetworks, combining specialized and shared experts.
en.result: After 20,000-step distributed pretraining, routing autonomously bifurcates: open-channel validation tokens route exclusively to Expert 0, porous-media tokens to Expert 1; latent validation MSEs of 2.46e-5 and 9.76e-6, decoded physical MSEs of 2.48e-6 and 1.76e-6, velocity divergence ~2.8e-10.
en.help_to_user: Provides a scalable sparse routing mechanism for building universal multi-physics neural operators that mitigate negative transfer while enforcing physical conservation.

idea_spark.idea: Embedding physical constraints (e.g., divergence-free) directly into latent representations with dynamic routing can be generalized to any conservation-law PDE system.
idea_spark.risk: Expert routing may introduce load imbalance or overfitting; embedding physical constraints requires customization per PDE, limiting generality.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by sparse MoE in NLP, combined with physics-informed encoding to achieve automatic domain bifurcation in multi-physics training.

papers.title: Randomized Atomic Feature Models for Physics-Informed Identification of Dynamic Systems
papers.summary: We present a physics-informed framework for system identification based on randomized stable atomic features. Impulse responses are represented as random superpositions of stable atoms, namely damped complex exponentials associated with poles sampled inside a prescribed disk. Identification is then cast as a convex regularized least-squares problem with optional linear, second-order-cone, and KYP constraints. The approach generalizes random Fourier and random Laplace features to the damped, nonstationary regime relevant to engineering systems while retaining modal interpretability and scalable finite-dimensional computation. The main analytic point is an operator-theoretic Disk-Bochner viewpoint: positive measures over stable poles generate positive-definite kernels with a radius-dependent shift defect, while a converse scalar disk moment representation for an arbitrary kernel is characterized by subnormality of the canonical shift. We prove this statement, establish an RKHS-to-l1 embedding, show that sampled poles induce a valid finite atomic gauge, discuss random-feature convergence, and state sparse-recovery guarantees conditionally on the restricted-eigenvalue properties of the realized disk-Vandermonde or input-output design matrix. We also connect the normalized transfer function problem to Nevanlinna-Pick interpolation and LFT set-membership. The framework directly encodes stability margins, modal localization, DC-gain bounds, monotonicity, passivity, relative degree, settling-time targets, and time/frequency-domain error bounds. Numerical comparisons illustrate how physically meaningful priors can compensate for poor excitation and improve constrained impulse-response recovery in an under-informative data setting.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于随机稳定原子特征的物理信息框架，用于动态系统辨识，将脉冲响应表示为阻尼复指数随机叠加，通过凸正则化最小二乘求解，并引入多种物理约束。
ai.motivation: 传统系统辨识方法难以融合物理先验，且在高维或数据不足时性能下降。
ai.method: 将脉冲响应表示为随机采样的稳定极点对应的阻尼复指数叠加，将辨识转化为带线性、二阶锥和KYP约束的凸正则化最小二乘问题，并建立盘-博赫纳算子理论框架。
ai.result: 数值实验表明，物理先验能补偿激励不足，改善约束脉冲响应恢复效果。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种可扩展、可解释且能融入多种物理约束的系统辨识新方法。

idea_spark.idea: 将随机特征与物理约束（如稳定性、无源性）结合，实现数据高效且可解释的模型学习。
idea_spark.risk: 随机采样极点可能引入近似误差，且约束优化求解计算成本较高。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受随机傅里叶特征和拉普拉斯特征启发，将其扩展至阻尼非平稳系统辨识。

en.tldr: A physics-informed framework for system identification using randomized stable atomic features, representing impulse responses as random superpositions of damped complex exponentials and solving a convex regularized least-squares problem with physical constraints.
en.motivation: Traditional system identification methods struggle to incorporate physical priors and perform poorly with limited or poor excitation data.
en.method: Impulse responses are modeled as random superpositions of stable poles (damped complex exponentials), and identification is cast as a convex regularized least-squares problem with linear, second-order cone, and KYP constraints, supported by a disk-Bochner operator-theoretic viewpoint.
en.result: Numerical experiments show that physical priors compensate for poor excitation and improve constrained impulse-response recovery in under-informative data settings.
en.help_to_user: Provides a scalable, interpretable system identification framework that can incorporate diverse physical constraints.

idea_spark.idea: Combine random features with physical constraints (e.g., stability, passivity) for data-efficient and interpretable model learning.
idea_spark.risk: Random pole sampling may introduce approximation errors, and constrained optimization can be computationally expensive.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by random Fourier and Laplace features, extended to damped nonstationary system identification.

papers.title: MetaGEM: Bottom-Up Reconstruction of Genome-Scale Metabolic Networks via Deep Enzyme-Metabolite Anchoring
papers.summary: Genome-scale metabolic models (GEMs) are essential tools for systems biology and rational chassis design, but conventional top-down reconstruction depends heavily on sequence homology and often leaves unknown enzymes and metabolic dark matter unresolved. Direct reconstruction from metabolomics is also difficult because mapping observed metabolites to reactions is an ill-posed inverse problem with combinatorial ambiguity and possible spurious networks. Here we present MetaGEM, a bottom-up framework that uses enzymes as physical anchors to convert system-level network inference into enzyme-metabolite interaction prediction. MetaGEM uses a multimodal dual-tower architecture that combines protein evolutionary semantics from a protein language model with three-dimensional metabolite representations. It further introduces contrastive learning with hard negative mining to separate structurally similar metabolites and reduce false positive interactions. On a de-homologized benchmark, MetaGEM achieves state-of-the-art enzyme-metabolite prediction performance, with AUROC of 0.9701 and MCC of 0.8033, and remains robust under low sequence identity splits. In downstream reconstruction, MetaGEM generates functional genome-scale metabolic models for Escherichia coli, Bacillus subtilis, and Pseudomonas aeruginosa. The reconstructed models improve network connectivity, capture promiscuous enzymes, and show strong agreement with experimental phenotype microarray and gene essentiality data. These results indicate that MetaGEM provides a practical route from metabolomic evidence to computable metabolic networks and offers a foundation for automated AI-driven virtual cell reconstruction.

ai.tldr: 提出MetaGEM，一种通过深度酶-代谢物锚定从代谢组学数据自下而上重建基因组规模代谢网络的方法。
ai.motivation: 传统自上而下的代谢网络重建依赖序列同源性，无法解析未知酶和代谢暗物质；直接利用代谢组学重建又面临组合歧义和假阳性网络问题。
ai.method: 采用多模态双塔架构，结合蛋白质语言模型和三维代谢物表征，并通过对比学习与困难负样本挖掘区分结构相似的代谢物。
ai.result: 在去同源性基准上AUROC达0.9701，MCC达0.8033；成功重建大肠杆菌、枯草芽孢杆菌和铜绿假单胞菌的功能性基因组规模代谢模型，与实验表型微阵列和基因必需性数据高度一致。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究人员从代谢组学证据直接构建可计算的代谢网络，尤其适用于未知酶和代谢暗物质场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将酶作为物理锚点，将网络推断转化为酶-代谢物相互作用预测，避免直接求解逆问题。
idea_spark.risk: 对代谢物三维表征的依赖可能限制在缺乏结构数据的代谢物上的应用；对比学习中的困难负样本挖掘可能引入偏差。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受蛋白质语言模型和三维分子表示在生物信息学中成功应用的启发，结合两者解决代谢网络重建中的组合歧义问题。

en.tldr: MetaGEM is a bottom-up framework that reconstructs genome-scale metabolic networks by converting network inference into enzyme-metabolite interaction prediction using a multimodal dual-tower architecture and contrastive learning.
en.motivation: Conventional top-down reconstruction relies on sequence homology and fails to resolve unknown enzymes and metabolic dark matter; direct metabolomics-based reconstruction suffers from combinatorial ambiguity and spurious networks.
en.method: Uses a multimodal dual-tower architecture combining protein language model embeddings with 3D metabolite representations, and employs contrastive learning with hard negative mining to distinguish structurally similar metabolites.
en.result: Achieves state-of-the-art enzyme-metabolite prediction (AUROC 0.9701, MCC 0.8033) on a de-homologized benchmark; reconstructs functional GEMs for E. coli, B. subtilis, and P. aeruginosa with strong agreement with experimental data.
en.help_to_user: Enables researchers to build computable metabolic networks directly from metabolomic evidence, especially useful for unknown enzymes and metabolic dark matter.

idea_spark.idea: Use enzymes as physical anchors to convert network inference into enzyme-metabolite interaction prediction, avoiding the ill-posed inverse problem.
idea_spark.risk: Dependence on 3D metabolite representations may limit applicability to metabolites without structural data; hard negative mining in contrastive learning may introduce bias.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the success of protein language models and 3D molecular representations in bioinformatics, combining them to address combinatorial ambiguity in metabolic network reconstruction.

keywords_raw: contrastive learning with hard negative mining

papers.title: Watch your neighbors: Training statistically accurate chaotic systems with local phase space information
papers.summary: Chaotic systems pose fundamental challenges for data-driven dynamics discovery, as small modeling errors lead to exponentially growing trajectory discrepancies. Since exact long-term prediction is unattainable, it is natural to ask what a good surrogate model for chaotic dynamics is. Prior work has largely focused either on reproducing the Jacobian of the underlying dynamics, which governs local expansion and contraction rates, or on training surrogate models that reproduce the ground-truth dynamics' long-term statistical behavior. In this work, we propose a new framework that aims to bridge these two paradigms by training surrogate dynamics models with accurate Jacobians and long-term statistical properties. Our method constructs a local covering of a chaotic attractor in phase space and analyzes the expansion and contraction of these coverings under the dynamics. The surrogate model is trained by minimizing the maximum mean discrepancy between the pushforward distributions of the coverings under the surrogate and ground-truth dynamics. Experiments show that our method significantly improves Jacobian accuracy while remaining competitive with state-of-the-art statistically accurate dynamics learning methods. Our code is fully available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/neighborwatch.

ai.tldr: 提出一种新框架，通过局部相空间覆盖和最大均值差异训练代理模型，同时提高雅可比矩阵精度和长期统计特性。
ai.motivation: 混沌系统建模中，小误差导致轨迹指数级偏离，现有方法要么关注雅可比矩阵精度，要么关注长期统计行为，但两者难以兼顾。
ai.method: 构建混沌吸引子的局部相空间覆盖，分析覆盖在动力学下的膨胀与收缩，通过最小化代理模型与真实动力学之间覆盖推前分布的最大均值差异来训练模型。
ai.result: 该方法显著提升了雅可比矩阵精度，同时在统计精度上与最先进方法保持竞争力。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何同时优化混沌系统代理模型的局部动力学精度和全局统计行为。

idea_spark.idea: 利用相空间局部覆盖的膨胀收缩信息，结合分布匹配损失，实现局部与全局性能的平衡。
idea_spark.risk: 局部覆盖的构造和推前分布计算可能在高维系统中计算成本过高。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从动力系统理论中局部线性化与全局统计的桥梁需求出发，启发将几何信息融入数据驱动损失函数。

en.tldr: Proposes a framework that trains surrogate models for chaotic systems by matching local phase space coverings via maximum mean discrepancy, improving Jacobian accuracy while maintaining long-term statistical fidelity.
en.motivation: Small modeling errors in chaotic systems cause exponentially growing trajectory discrepancies; existing methods focus either on Jacobian accuracy or long-term statistics, but not both.
en.method: Constructs a local covering of the chaotic attractor in phase space, analyzes expansion/contraction of coverings under dynamics, and trains the surrogate by minimizing MMD between pushforward distributions of coverings under surrogate and ground-truth dynamics.
en.result: Significantly improves Jacobian accuracy while remaining competitive with state-of-the-art statistically accurate dynamics learning methods.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to simultaneously optimize local dynamics accuracy and global statistical behavior in surrogate models for chaotic systems.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage expansion/contraction information from local phase space coverings combined with distribution matching loss to balance local and global performance.
idea_spark.risk: Construction of local coverings and computation of pushforward distributions may be computationally expensive in high-dimensional systems.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the need to bridge local linearization and global statistics in dynamical systems theory, suggesting incorporating geometric information into data-driven loss functions.

papers.title: ViT-K: A Few-Shot Learning Model for Coupled Fluid-Porous Media Flows with Interface Conditions
papers.summary: The numerical simulation of interaction between free flow and porous media, governed by coupled Stokes/Navier--Stokes--Darcy flows, is critical for understanding fluid filtration and physiological transport, yet it is hindered by the high computational cost of resolving interface heterogeneities and the instability of long-term predictions. While deep learning offers surrogate modeling potential, existing frameworks often suffer from exponential error accumulation and poor convergence in multi-physics regimes. To address these limitations, we propose ViT-K, a novel few-shot learning model designed to learn the spatiotemporal evolution of coupled flows from sparse datasets. The ViT-K framework effectively reconstructs the global flow physics on a low-dimensional manifold by combining Vision Transformers (ViT) to capture heterogeneous interfacial features with the Koopman operator to linearize temporal dynamics. By lifting nonlinear dynamics into a globally linear observable space, the ViT-K model provides stability by design, ensuring that prediction errors grow linearly rather than exponentially over time. This theoretical property enables reliable long-term extrapolation even in small-sample regimes. Numerical experiments on benchmark coupled systems demonstrate that ViT-K not only captures complex interface physics with high fidelity but also exhibits exceptional robustness against measurement noise by acting as an implicit spectral filter. The proposed method significantly outperforms traditional solvers in inference speed while maintaining physical consistency, offering a robust paradigm for real-time multiphysics forecasting.

ai.tldr: 提出ViT-K模型，结合Vision Transformer与Koopman算子，从稀疏数据中学习耦合自由流与多孔介质流动的时空演化，实现稳定长期预测。
ai.motivation: 传统数值模拟计算成本高、长期预测不稳定；现有深度学习代理模型误差指数累积、收敛性差。
ai.method: 利用Vision Transformer捕获界面异质特征，Koopman算子将非线性动力学线性化，在低维流形上重构全局流动物理。
ai.result: 在基准耦合系统上，ViT-K高保真捕捉界面物理，误差线性增长而非指数，对测量噪声鲁棒，推理速度远超传统求解器。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种适用于小样本、多物理场实时预测的稳健深度学习范式。

zh.motivation: 传统数值模拟计算成本高、长期预测不稳定；现有深度学习代理模型误差指数累积、收敛性差。

en.tldr: Proposes ViT-K, combining Vision Transformer and Koopman operator to learn spatiotemporal evolution of coupled free-flow and porous media flows from sparse data, enabling stable long-term predictions.
en.motivation: Traditional numerical simulations are computationally expensive and unstable for long-term predictions; existing deep learning surrogates suffer from exponential error accumulation and poor convergence.
en.method: Uses Vision Transformer to capture heterogeneous interfacial features and Koopman operator to linearize nonlinear dynamics, reconstructing global flow physics on a low-dimensional manifold.
en.result: On benchmark coupled systems, ViT-K captures interface physics with high fidelity, achieves linear error growth, is robust to measurement noise, and significantly outperforms traditional solvers in inference speed.
en.help_to_user: Provides a robust deep learning paradigm for real-time multiphysics forecasting with limited data.

idea_spark.idea: Combine Koopman operator with Transformer to leverage linearized dynamics for long-term stability while preserving spatial heterogeneous features.
idea_spark.risk: Koopman linearization may fail for highly nonlinear or chaotic systems; ViT training on sparse data may overfit.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by Koopman theory in dynamical systems, combined with attention mechanisms to handle multi-scale interfacial features.

keywords_raw: free flow and porous media interaction

papers.title: Average Gradient Outer Product in kernel regression provably recovers the central subspace for multi-index models
papers.summary: We study a prototypical situation when a learned predictor can discover useful low-dimensional structure in data, while using fewer samples than are needed for accurate prediction. Specifically, we consider the problem of recovering a multi-index polynomial $f^*(x)=h(Ux)$, with $U\in\mathbb{R}^{r\times d}$ and $r\ll d$, from finitely many data/label pairs. Importantly, the target function depends on input $x$ only through the projection onto an unknown $r$-dimensional central subspace. The algorithm we analyze is appealingly simple: fit kernel ridge regression (KRR) to the data and compute the Average Gradient Outer Product (AGOP) from the fitted predictor. Our main results show that under reasonable assumptions the top $r$-dimensional eigenspace of AGOP provably recovers the central subspace, even in regimes when the prediction error remains large. Specifically, if the target function $f^*$ has degree $p^*$, it is known that $n\asymp d^{p^*}$ samples are necessary for KRR to achieve accurate prediction. In contrast, we show that if a low degree $p$ component of $f^*$ already carries all relevant directions for prediction, subspace recovery occurs in the much lower sample regime $n\asymp d^{p+δ}$ for any $δ\in(0,1)$. Our results thus demonstrate a separation between prediction and representation, and provide an explanation for why iterative kernel methods such as Recursive Feature Machines (RFM) can be sample-efficient in practice.

ai.tldr: 研究证明，在核岭回归中，平均梯度外积（AGOP）可以在预测误差仍较大时，以远低于准确预测所需的样本量，恢复多指标模型中的中心子空间。
ai.motivation: 探索学习器如何利用比准确预测所需更少的样本，发现数据中有效的低维结构。
ai.method: 对数据拟合核岭回归，然后从拟合的预测器中计算平均梯度外积（AGOP），取其前r维特征空间作为中心子空间的估计。
ai.result: 在目标函数为多项式且存在低阶成分包含所有相关方向时，子空间恢复所需的样本量仅为n ≍ d^{p+δ}，远低于准确预测所需的n ≍ d^{p*}。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解为什么迭代核方法（如递归特征机）在实践中能够样本高效地学习低维表示。

idea_spark.idea: 将AGOP与自适应基函数结合，设计更高效的迭代特征学习算法。
idea_spark.risk: 理论假设（多项式目标、低阶成分主导）在实际数据中可能不成立，导致恢复失败。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受预测与表示分离现象的启发，可探索其他统计量（如Hessian）在低样本下的子空间恢复能力。

en.tldr: We show that in kernel ridge regression, the Average Gradient Outer Product (AGOP) can provably recover the central subspace of multi-index models with far fewer samples than needed for accurate prediction.
en.motivation: To understand how a learned predictor can discover useful low-dimensional structure in data while using fewer samples than required for accurate prediction.
en.method: Fit kernel ridge regression to the data, then compute the Average Gradient Outer Product (AGOP) from the fitted predictor and extract its top r-dimensional eigenspace.
en.result: When the target function has a low-degree component carrying all relevant directions, subspace recovery requires only n ≍ d^{p+δ} samples, much lower than the n ≍ d^{p*} needed for accurate prediction.
en.help_to_user: Explains why iterative kernel methods like Recursive Feature Machines can be sample-efficient in practice for learning low-dimensional representations.

idea_spark.idea: Combine AGOP with adaptive basis functions to design more efficient iterative feature learning algorithms.
idea_spark.risk: Theoretical assumptions (polynomial target, dominant low-degree component) may not hold in real data, leading to recovery failure.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the separation between prediction and representation, explore other statistics (e.g., Hessian) for subspace recovery under low sample regimes.

papers.title: A Novel Schur-Decomposition-Based Weight Projection Method for Stable State-Space Neural-Network Architectures
papers.summary: Building black-box models for dynamical systems from data is a challenging problem in machine learning, especially when asymptotic stability guarantees are required. In this paper, we introduce a novel stability-ensuring and backpropagation-compatible projection scheme based on the Schur decomposition for the state matrix of linear discrete-time state-space layers, as well as an alternative pre-factorized formulation of the methodology. The proposed methods dynamically project the quasi-triangular factor of the state matrix's real Schur decomposition onto its nearest stable peer, ensuring stable dynamics with minimal overparameterization. Experiments on synthetic linear systems demonstrate that the method achieves accuracy and convergence rates comparable to those of state-of-the-art stable-system identification techniques, despite a marginal increase in computational complexity. Furthermore, the lower weight count facilitates convergence during training without sacrificing accuracy in stacked neural-network architectures with static nonlinearities targeting real-world datasets. These results suggest that the Schur-based projection provides a numerically robust framework for identifying complex dynamics on par with the State of the Art while satisfying strict asymptotic-stability requirements.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于Schur分解的权重投影方法，确保线性离散时间状态空间层的渐近稳定性，同时保持反向传播兼容性。
ai.motivation: 从数据构建黑箱动力学模型时，保证渐近稳定性是一个挑战，现有方法存在过参数化或数值不稳定的问题。
ai.method: 利用实Schur分解将状态矩阵的拟三角因子动态投影到最近的稳定矩阵，并给出预因子化替代方案。
ai.result: 在合成线性系统上达到与最先进稳定系统识别方法相当的精度和收敛速度，计算复杂度略有增加；在堆叠神经网络中权重更少，训练收敛更快且不牺牲精度。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种数值鲁棒的框架，用于在满足严格渐近稳定性要求的同时识别复杂动力学。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将Schur投影与非线性状态空间模型结合，或用于连续时间系统的稳定性保证。
idea_spark.risk: Schur分解的计算开销在极大规模网络中可能成为瓶颈；投影可能引入额外偏差。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受线性系统理论中Schur分解的数值稳定性启发，将其用于神经网络训练中的动态约束。

en.tldr: Introduces a novel Schur-decomposition-based weight projection method to ensure asymptotic stability in linear discrete-time state-space layers while remaining backpropagation-compatible.
en.motivation: Building black-box dynamical system models from data with asymptotic stability guarantees is challenging; existing methods suffer from overparameterization or numerical instability.
en.method: Projects the quasi-triangular factor of the state matrix's real Schur decomposition onto its nearest stable counterpart, with an alternative pre-factorized formulation.
en.result: Achieves accuracy and convergence comparable to state-of-the-art stable system identification on synthetic linear systems with marginal computational overhead; reduces weight count in stacked networks without accuracy loss.
en.help_to_user: Provides a numerically robust framework for identifying complex dynamics under strict asymptotic stability requirements.

idea_spark.idea: Explore combining Schur projection with nonlinear state-space models or extending to continuous-time systems.
idea_spark.risk: Schur decomposition may become a computational bottleneck for very large networks; projection could introduce bias.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the numerical stability of Schur decomposition in linear system theory, applied as a dynamic constraint during neural network training.

papers.title: Runaway avalanches in plasmas with external electric fields: spatially inhomogeneous case in a perturbation framework
papers.summary: We consider the Landau-Coulomb equation for a (hydrogen) plasma heated by an external electric field. In this setting, theoretical and experimental results in plasma physics show the emergence of so-called \emph{runaway electrons} which are linearly accelerating but only lead to a minimal increase of the plasma temperature. Runaway electrons are a major obstacle in nuclear fusion since they can overcome the confinement and damage the structure of the reactor.   We rigorously prove the well-posedness of the underlying nonlinear \emph{open} Landau-Coulomb system in a perturbative setting and the conjectured growth bounds for the mean velocity and plasma temperature. We show that the mean velocity is linearly increasing in time, and capture the sharp logarithmic growth of the temperature. Furthermore, we prove that the electron distribution can be asymptotically described by a scattering-type Maxwellian. Due to the different nature of the electron-electron and electron-ion interactions, we recast the equation as a novel coupled system that allows us to isolate the dissipation structures of the two operators. For the coupled system, we perform a micro-macro decomposition to show convergence to the scattering-type Maxwellian.

ai.tldr: 本文在微扰框架下严格证明了外电场加热等离子体中逃逸电子的存在性，并给出了平均速度线性增长和温度对数增长的精确界。
ai.motivation: 核聚变中逃逸电子会损坏反应堆结构，需要从理论上理解其产生机制和演化规律。
ai.method: 将Landau-Coulomb方程重写为电子-电子和电子-离子耦合系统，利用微宏分解证明解收敛到散射型麦克斯韦分布。
ai.result: 平均速度随时间线性增长，温度呈对数增长，电子分布渐近为散射型麦克斯韦分布。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解外电场下等离子体中逃逸电子的数学理论，为核聚变装置设计提供理论依据。

idea_spark.idea: 将电子-离子碰撞视为散射过程，可能启发新的数值算法。
idea_spark.risk: 理论结果基于微扰假设，强场情形可能不适用。
idea_spark.inspiration: 逃逸电子的温度对数增长与某些湍流系统中的间歇性行为类似，可探索跨领域类比。

en.tldr: This paper rigorously proves the well-posedness of the Landau-Coulomb system with an external electric field in a perturbative setting, showing linear growth of mean velocity and logarithmic growth of temperature for runaway electrons.
en.motivation: Runaway electrons in nuclear fusion can damage reactor structures, necessitating a theoretical understanding of their generation and evolution.
en.method: The Landau-Coulomb equation is reformulated as a coupled electron-electron and electron-ion system, and a micro-macro decomposition is used to prove convergence to a scattering-type Maxwellian.
en.result: Mean velocity grows linearly in time, temperature grows logarithmically, and the electron distribution asymptotically approaches a scattering-type Maxwellian.
en.help_to_user: Provides a rigorous mathematical foundation for runaway electron dynamics in plasmas under external electric fields, aiding fusion reactor design.

idea_spark.idea: Treating electron-ion collisions as a scattering process may inspire new numerical algorithms.
idea_spark.risk: The perturbative assumption may break down for strong electric fields.
idea_spark.inspiration: The logarithmic temperature growth resembles intermittent behavior in turbulent systems, suggesting cross-disciplinary analogies.

papers.title: DiffPhD: A Unified Differentiable Solver for Projective Heterogeneous Materials in Elastodynamics with Contact-Rich GPU-Acceleration
papers.summary: Differentiable simulation of soft bodies is a foundation for system identification, trajectory optimization, and Real2Sim transfer. Yet, existing methods such as the differentiable Projective Dynamics (DiffPD) struggle when faced with heterogeneous materials with extreme stiffness contrasts, hyperelasticity under large deformations, and contact-rich interactions, which are common scenarios in the real world. We present DiffPhD, a unified GPU-accelerated differentiable Projective Dynamics framework for heterogeneous materials that tackles these intertwined challenges simultaneously. Our key insight is a careful integration of: (i) stiffness-aware projective weights to embed heterogeneity into the global system; (ii) trust-region eigenvalue filtering lifted to the backward pass for stable hyperelastic gradients and a type-II Anderson Acceleration scheme with dual-gate convergence to stabilize forward iteration under large stiffness contrasts; and (iii) a unified GPU pipeline that reuses a single sparse factor across forward, backward, and contact computations, with stiffness-amplified Rayleigh damping folded into the same factor for heterogeneity-aware dissipation at zero recurring cost. DiffPhD achieves strict gradient accuracy while delivering up to an order-of-magnitude speedup over prior differentiable solvers on heterogeneous, hyperelastic, contact-rich benchmarks. Crucially, this speedup does not come at the cost of stability: DiffPhD remains convergent on stiffness contrasts up to 100x where prior PD solvers degrade. This unlocks end-to-end gradient-based optimization on regimes previously bottlenecked by either solver fragility or per-iteration cost -- shell--joint composite creatures, soft characters wielding stiff weapons, and soft-gripper robotic manipulation -- all handled within a single forward--backward pass.

ai.tldr: 提出DiffPhD，一个统一的GPU加速可微投影动力学框架，能同时处理异质材料、大变形超弹性及密集接触场景，在保持梯度精度的同时实现数量级加速。
ai.motivation: 现有可微软体模拟方法（如DiffPD）在真实世界常见的异质材料、大变形超弹性和密集接触场景中表现不佳，面临稳定性差或计算成本高的问题。
ai.method: 集成刚度感知投影权重、后向传递中的信赖域特征值滤波、双门控Anderson加速，以及统一GPU管线（前向、后向、接触计算共享单一稀疏因子，并融入刚度放大瑞利阻尼）。
ai.result: 在异质、超弹性、密集接触基准测试中，相比先前可微求解器实现最高一个数量级的加速，且刚度对比度高达100倍时仍保持收敛。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过可微模拟实现系统辨识、轨迹优化和Real2Sim迁移，尤其适用于复杂材料与接触场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将信赖域特征值滤波与Anderson加速结合，同时稳定前向和后向梯度传播，为高对比度材料模拟提供新思路。
idea_spark.risk: 方法依赖投影动力学框架，可能不适用于非投影类模拟器；GPU管线优化需特定硬件支持。
idea_spark.inspiration: 统一GPU管线中共享稀疏因子的设计启发：在可微模拟中复用计算资源以降低开销，可推广至多物理场耦合场景。

en.tldr: DiffPhD is a unified GPU-accelerated differentiable Projective Dynamics framework that simultaneously handles heterogeneous materials, large-deformation hyperelasticity, and contact-rich interactions, achieving up to an order-of-magnitude speedup while maintaining gradient accuracy.
en.motivation: Existing differentiable soft-body simulators (e.g., DiffPD) struggle with real-world scenarios involving heterogeneous materials, extreme stiffness contrasts, hyperelasticity under large deformations, and dense contact, suffering from instability or high per-iteration cost.
en.method: Integrates stiffness-aware projective weights, trust-region eigenvalue filtering in the backward pass, dual-gate Anderson Acceleration, and a unified GPU pipeline that reuses a single sparse factor across forward, backward, and contact computations with stiffness-amplified Rayleigh damping.
en.result: Achieves up to an order-of-magnitude speedup over prior differentiable solvers on heterogeneous, hyperelastic, contact-rich benchmarks, and remains convergent for stiffness contrasts up to 100x where prior methods degrade.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how differentiable simulation enables system identification, trajectory optimization, and Real2Sim transfer, especially for complex materials and contact scenarios.

idea_spark.idea: Combining trust-region eigenvalue filtering with Anderson Acceleration to stabilize both forward and backward gradient propagation offers a new approach for high-contrast material simulation.
idea_spark.risk: The method relies on the Projective Dynamics framework and may not apply to non-projective simulators; GPU pipeline optimization requires specific hardware.
idea_spark.inspiration: The unified GPU pipeline's reuse of a single sparse factor inspires cost reduction in differentiable simulation by sharing computations, which could be extended to multi-physics coupling.

papers.title: Wahkon: A Statistically Principled Deep RKHS Superposition Network
papers.summary: Deep learning excels at prediction but often lacks finite-sample guarantees and calibrated uncertainty; RKHS (Reproducing Kernel Hilbert Space)-based methods provide those guarantees but struggle to adapt in high dimensions. We propose Wahkon, a deep RKHS superposition network that unifies Kolmogorov's superposition principle with RKHS regularization in the smoothing-spline tradition of Wahba. This yields a finite-dimensional deep representer theorem that makes training tractable and provides explicit layerwise complexity control. We show the penalized estimator is exactly the MAP (maximum a posteriori) estimate under a hierarchical Gaussian-process prior, extending the spline/GP duality to deep compositions. Using metric-entropy arguments, we establish minimax-optimal convergence rates under mild smoothness and clarify how depth and width trade off with regularity. Empirically, Wahkon outperforms multilayer perceptrons, Neural Tangent Kernels, and Kolmogorov--Arnold Networks across simulation benchmarks and a single-cell CITE-seq study. By unifying Kolmogorov's superposition principle with RKHS regularization, Wahkon delivers accuracy, interpretability, and statistical rigor in a single framework.

ai.tldr: 提出Wahkon，一种融合Kolmogorov叠加原理与RKHS正则化的深度网络，兼具预测精度与统计保证。
ai.motivation: 深度学习预测能力强但缺乏有限样本保证和校准的不确定性；RKHS方法提供保证但高维适应困难。
ai.method: 将Kolmogorov叠加原理与Wahba平滑样条传统中的RKHS正则化统一，导出有限维深度表示定理，并建立分层高斯过程先验下的MAP估计。
ai.result: 在模拟基准和单细胞CITE-seq研究中优于MLP、NTK和KAN；获得极小极大最优收敛率。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种兼具统计严谨性与深度学习灵活性的框架，适用于需要可解释性和不确定性量化的高维预测任务。

idea_spark.idea: 将Kolmogorov叠加定理与RKHS正则化结合，实现深度网络的统计可解释性。
idea_spark.risk: 理论假设（如平滑性）可能限制实际复杂数据中的表现。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从Wahba平滑样条与Kolmogorov定理的交叉点出发，构建统计与深度学习的桥梁。

en.tldr: Proposes Wahkon, a deep RKHS superposition network unifying Kolmogorov's superposition principle with RKHS regularization, achieving both predictive accuracy and statistical guarantees.
en.motivation: Deep learning excels at prediction but lacks finite-sample guarantees and calibrated uncertainty; RKHS methods provide guarantees but struggle in high dimensions.
en.method: Unifies Kolmogorov's superposition principle with RKHS regularization in the smoothing-spline tradition, yielding a finite-dimensional deep representer theorem and MAP estimation under a hierarchical Gaussian-process prior.
en.result: Outperforms MLP, NTK, and KAN on simulation benchmarks and a single-cell CITE-seq study; achieves minimax-optimal convergence rates.
en.help_to_user: Provides a framework that combines statistical rigor with deep learning flexibility, suitable for high-dimensional prediction tasks requiring interpretability and uncertainty quantification.

idea_spark.idea: Combining Kolmogorov's superposition theorem with RKHS regularization to achieve statistical interpretability in deep networks.
idea_spark.risk: Theoretical assumptions (e.g., smoothness) may limit performance on complex real-world data.
idea_spark.inspiration: Bridging statistics and deep learning from the intersection of Wahba's smoothing splines and Kolmogorov's theorem.

papers.title: A general classification of the replication dynamics with a unique fixed point in the interior of simplex $S_N$
papers.summary: The replication dynamics (differential equation system) is the foundation of evolutionary game theory. When n=2, there are four possible types of replication dynamics. When n=3, there are 49 possible types of replication dynamics. However, when n>3, the classification of replication dynamics has not been solved. In this article, the sufficient and necessary conditions of the replication dynamics equation with a unique fixed point in the interior of simplex $S_n$(Int$S_n$) for $n\geq 2$ are presented. Furthermore, the different types of replication dynamics equations with a unique fixed point in IntSn is discussed.

ai.tldr: 本文给出了单纯形内部存在唯一不动点的复制动力学方程的充要条件，并讨论了其不同类型。
ai.motivation: 当n>3时，复制动力学的分类问题尚未解决，本文旨在填补这一空白。
ai.method: 通过数学推导，给出n≥2时复制动力学方程在单纯形内部存在唯一不动点的充要条件，并分类讨论。
ai.result: 得到了复制动力学方程在单纯形内部存在唯一不动点的充要条件，并描述了不同类型。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解高维复制动力学的分类和不动点性质。

idea_spark.idea: 将复制动力学的分类结果应用于多策略博弈的稳定性分析。
idea_spark.risk: 高维情况下的分类可能过于复杂，实际应用需简化。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用不动点条件设计新的博弈策略或控制演化路径。

en.tldr: This paper presents sufficient and necessary conditions for replication dynamics with a unique fixed point in the interior of the simplex, and discusses its different types.
en.motivation: The classification of replication dynamics for n>3 remains unsolved; this work aims to fill that gap.
en.method: Mathematical derivation of necessary and sufficient conditions for a unique interior fixed point in replication dynamics for n≥2, followed by classification.
en.result: Obtained the conditions for a unique interior fixed point and described the different types.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand the classification and fixed-point properties of high-dimensional replication dynamics.

idea_spark.idea: Apply the classification results to stability analysis of multi-strategy games.
idea_spark.risk: High-dimensional classification may be too complex for practical applications.
idea_spark.inspiration: Use fixed-point conditions to design new game strategies or control evolutionary paths.

papers.title: Critic-Driven Voronoi-Quantization for Distilling Deep RL Policies to Explainable Models
papers.summary: Despite many successful attempts at explaining Deep Reinforcement Learning policies using distillation, it remains difficult to balance the performance-interpretability trade-off and select a fitting surrogate model. In addition to this, traditional distillation only minimizes the distance between the behavior of the original and the surrogate policy while other RL-specific components such as action value are disregarded. To solve this, we introduce a new model-agnostic method called Critic-Driven Voronoi State Partitioning, which partitions a black box control policy into regions where a simple class of model can be optimized using gradient descent. By exploiting the critic value network of the original policy, we iteratively introduce new subpolicies in regions with insufficient value, standing in for a measure of policy complexity. The partitioning, a Voronoi quantizer, uses nearest neighbor lookups to assign a linear function to each point in the state space resulting in a cell-like diagram. We validate our approach on several well known benchmarks and proof that this distillation approaches the original policy using a reasonable sized set of linear functions.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于评论家的Voronoi状态划分方法，将深度强化学习策略蒸馏为可解释的线性子策略集合，平衡性能与可解释性。
ai.motivation: 现有蒸馏方法难以平衡性能与可解释性，且忽略了动作价值等强化学习特有信息。
ai.method: 利用原始策略的评论家价值网络，在价值不足的区域迭代引入新的线性子策略，通过Voronoi量化器将状态空间划分为细胞状区域，每个区域分配一个线性函数。
ai.result: 在多个基准测试上验证，证明该方法能用合理数量的线性函数逼近原始策略。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种将黑盒强化学习策略转化为可解释模型的具体技术，有助于理解策略决策逻辑。

idea_spark.idea: 结合价值网络与Voronoi划分，动态增加子策略以覆盖价值不足区域，实现自适应复杂度。
idea_spark.risk: 线性子策略可能无法处理高度非线性的状态空间，且Voronoi划分在高维空间中效率下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受强化学习中评论家网络指导策略优化的启发，将其用于蒸馏过程的可解释性分区。

zh.tldr: 提出一种基于评论家的Voronoi状态划分方法，将深度强化学习策略蒸馏为可解释的线性子策略集合，平衡性能与可解释性。

en.tldr: Proposes a critic-driven Voronoi state partitioning method to distill deep RL policies into explainable linear sub-policies, balancing performance and interpretability.
en.motivation: Existing distillation methods struggle to balance performance and interpretability, and ignore RL-specific components like action values.
en.method: Exploits the critic value network of the original policy to iteratively introduce new linear sub-policies in regions with insufficient value, using a Voronoi quantizer to partition the state space into cell-like regions each assigned a linear function.
en.result: Validated on several benchmarks, showing that the distillation approaches the original policy with a reasonable number of linear functions.
en.help_to_user: Provides a concrete technique to convert black-box RL policies into interpretable models, aiding understanding of policy decision logic.

idea_spark.idea: Combine value networks with Voronoi partitioning to dynamically add sub-policies in low-value regions, achieving adaptive complexity.
idea_spark.risk: Linear sub-policies may struggle with highly nonlinear state spaces, and Voronoi partitioning becomes inefficient in high dimensions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the use of critic networks to guide policy optimization in RL, applied here to interpretable partitioning during distillation.

papers.title: Stochastic Mackey-Glass Equations and Other Negative Feedback Systems: Existence of Invariant Measures
papers.summary: We study equations like the Mackey-Glass equations and Nicholson's blowflies equation, each perturbed by a (small) multiplicative noise term. Solutions to these stochastic negative feedback systems persist globally and are bounded above in probability under mild assumptions. A non-trivial invariant measure is proved to exist if and only if there is at least one initial condition for which the solution remains bounded away from zero in probability. The noise driving the dynamical system is allowed to be a square integrable Lévy process with finite intensity. Existence of invariant measures is obtained via the Krylov-Bogoliubov method. In addition to our theoretical results, we present numerical simulations identifying the invariant measures obtained via the Krylov-Bogoliubov method and illustrating their connection to the system's long-term behaviour.

ai.tldr: 研究随机Mackey-Glass方程等负反馈系统，证明在温和条件下存在不变测度，并通过数值模拟验证。
ai.motivation: 理解随机负反馈系统的长期行为，特别是不变测度的存在性条件。
ai.method: 使用Krylov-Bogoliubov方法，结合Lévy噪声的平方可积性，分析解的有界性和不变测度存在性。
ai.result: 不变测度存在当且仅当存在初始条件使解在概率上远离零；数值模拟展示了不变测度与长期行为的关系。
ai.help_to_user: 为随机延迟微分方程的不变测度理论提供严格分析框架和数值验证方法。

idea_spark.idea: 利用Krylov-Bogoliubov方法结合Lévy噪声，研究更复杂反馈系统的长期统计特性。
idea_spark.risk: 条件“解远离零”在实际系统中可能难以验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 数值模拟与理论结合的方法可启发其他随机动力系统的研究。

zh.tldr: 研究随机Mackey-Glass方程等负反馈系统，证明在温和条件下存在不变测度，并通过数值模拟验证。

idea_spark.risk: 条件“解远离零”在实际系统中可能难以验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 数值模拟与理论结合的方法可启发其他随机动力系统的研究。

en.tldr: Studies stochastic Mackey-Glass equations and similar negative feedback systems, proving existence of invariant measures under mild conditions, with numerical simulations.
en.motivation: To understand long-term behavior of stochastic negative feedback systems, especially conditions for invariant measures.
en.method: Uses Krylov-Bogoliubov method with square-integrable Lévy noise, analyzing boundedness and invariant measure existence.
en.result: Invariant measure exists iff there is an initial condition keeping the solution bounded away from zero in probability; simulations illustrate connection to long-term behavior.
en.help_to_user: Provides rigorous analysis and numerical validation for invariant measure theory in stochastic delay differential equations.

idea_spark.idea: Apply Krylov-Bogoliubov method with Lévy noise to study long-term statistics of more complex feedback systems.
idea_spark.risk: Condition 'bounded away from zero' may be hard to verify in practice.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining theory with numerical simulation can inspire research on other stochastic dynamical systems.

papers.title: Language Generation as Optimal Control: Closed-Loop Diffusion in Latent Control Space
papers.summary: This work reformulates language generation as a stochastic optimal control problem, providing a unified theoretical perspective to analyze autoregressive and diffusion models and explain their limitations (Efficiency-Fidelity Paradox, Irreversibility Error Propagation, Optimization Tractability and Fidelity) in terms of combination of trajectory singularity, adjoint state vanishing, and gradient absence. To address these issues, we approximate the solution to the Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman (HJB) equation, yielding an optimal policy that acts as a closed-loop controller. To bypass the intractability of directly solving the HJB PDE, we employ Flow Matching as the optimal trajectory solver within the rectified latent control space. This allows our Manta-LM with Global Integral Operator to approximate the global vector field, effectively realizing a model that simultaneously achieves high-fidelity text generation and efficient, low-cost parallel sampling. Empirically, our method achieves strong performance on language modeling and conditional generation tasks, while exhibiting improved stability, efficiency, and controllability.

ai.tldr: 将语言生成建模为随机最优控制问题，通过求解HJB方程得到闭环控制器，结合流匹配和全局积分算子实现高保真、高效并行的文本生成。
ai.motivation: 现有自回归和扩散模型存在效率-保真度悖论、不可逆误差传播、优化可解性及保真度等问题，缺乏统一理论视角。
ai.method: 将语言生成转化为随机最优控制问题，近似求解HJB方程得到最优闭环策略；在修正的潜在控制空间中用流匹配作为最优轨迹求解器，并通过全局积分算子Manta-LM逼近全局向量场。
ai.result: 在语言建模和条件生成任务上取得强性能，同时展现出更好的稳定性、效率和可控性。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种将语言生成与最优控制理论结合的新视角，有助于理解现有模型的局限并设计更高效的生成方法。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将HJB方程求解与扩散模型结合，在潜在空间中实现闭环控制，可能启发新的生成范式。
idea_spark.risk: HJB方程的高维求解仍具挑战，流匹配的近似可能引入偏差；闭环控制器的泛化性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受最优控制理论启发，将生成过程视为动态系统控制，可借鉴控制领域的成熟方法（如LQR、MPC）改进生成模型。

zh.tldr: 将语言生成建模为随机最优控制问题，通过求解HJB方程得到闭环控制器，结合流匹配和全局积分算子实现高保真、高效并行的文本生成。
zh.motivation: 现有自回归和扩散模型存在效率-保真度悖论、不可逆误差传播、优化可解性及保真度等问题，缺乏统一理论视角。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将HJB方程求解与扩散模型结合，在潜在空间中实现闭环控制，可能启发新的生成范式。
idea_spark.risk: HJB方程的高维求解仍具挑战，流匹配的近似可能引入偏差；闭环控制器的泛化性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受最优控制理论启发，将生成过程视为动态系统控制，可借鉴控制领域的成熟方法（如LQR、MPC）改进生成模型。

en.tldr: Formulates language generation as a stochastic optimal control problem, solves the HJB equation to obtain a closed-loop controller, and combines flow matching with a global integral operator for high-fidelity and efficient parallel text generation.
en.motivation: Existing autoregressive and diffusion models suffer from the Efficiency-Fidelity Paradox, Irreversibility Error Propagation, and optimization tractability issues, lacking a unified theoretical perspective.
en.method: Reformulates language generation as a stochastic optimal control problem, approximates the HJB equation solution to derive an optimal closed-loop policy; uses flow matching as the optimal trajectory solver in a rectified latent control space, and employs a global integral operator (Manta-LM) to approximate the global vector field.
en.result: Achieves strong performance on language modeling and conditional generation tasks, with improved stability, efficiency, and controllability.
en.help_to_user: Provides a new perspective linking language generation to optimal control theory, helping to understand limitations of current models and design more efficient generation methods.

idea_spark.idea: Explore combining HJB equation solving with diffusion models for closed-loop control in latent space, potentially inspiring new generation paradigms.
idea_spark.risk: High-dimensional HJB solving remains challenging; flow matching approximations may introduce bias; generalization of the closed-loop controller needs further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by optimal control theory, viewing generation as dynamic system control can leverage mature control methods (e.g., LQR, MPC) to improve generative models.

papers.title: Asymptotically Self-Similar Blowup for 3D Incompressible Euler with $C^{1, 1/3-}$ Velocity I: $C^{\infty}$ 1D Limiting Profiles
papers.summary: We consider a one-parameter family of 1D models for the 3D axisymmetric incompressible Euler equation with $C^α$ vorticity and without swirl near the symmetry axis. For $α= \frac13$, we impose a crucial normalization and construct a $C^{\infty}$ self-similar blowup profile with unbounded 1D stream function and infinite spatial blowup rate, using a fixed-point argument around a numerically constructed approximate profile. For $α< \frac13$ sufficiently close to $\frac13$, we perturb the $\frac13$-profile and analytically construct exact smooth 1D profiles with bounded stream function and finite spatial blowup rate. In the companion work~\cite{chen2026eulerII}, for any $α\in (0,\frac13)$, we lift these 1D blowup profiles to construct exact $C^{1,α}$ self-similar blowup profiles for 3D Euler, and build on them to prove sharp asymptotically self-similar blowup for 3D axisymmetric Euler without swirl from $C_c^α$ initial vorticity and $C^{1,α} \cap L^2$ initial velocity.

ai.tldr: 本文针对三维轴对称无旋不可压缩欧拉方程，构造了C^∞自相似爆破剖面，并分析了不同正则性指数下的爆破行为。
ai.motivation: 研究三维欧拉方程在有限时间内的爆破机制，特别是自相似爆破剖面的存在性与正则性。
ai.method: 通过一参数族一维模型，利用不动点论证和数值构造的近似剖面，解析构造光滑自相似爆破剖面。
ai.result: 对于α=1/3，构造了C^∞自相似爆破剖面，具有无界流函数和无限空间爆破率；对于α<1/3，构造了有界流函数和有限空间爆破率的光滑剖面。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解三维欧拉方程自相似爆破的数学构造和正则性条件。

idea_spark.idea: 利用数值近似剖面结合解析扰动，构造精确自相似解。
idea_spark.risk: 数值近似剖面的精度和收敛性需严格验证，且推广到更高维度可能面临技术困难。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从一维模型出发，逐步提升维度，为复杂PDE的奇性构造提供模块化思路。

en.tldr: This paper constructs C^∞ self-similar blowup profiles for the 3D axisymmetric incompressible Euler equation without swirl, and analyzes blowup behavior under different regularity exponents.
en.motivation: To investigate finite-time blowup mechanisms for the 3D Euler equations, particularly the existence and regularity of self-similar blowup profiles.
en.method: Using a one-parameter family of 1D models, a fixed-point argument around a numerically constructed approximate profile yields analytic smooth self-similar blowup profiles.
en.result: For α=1/3, a C^∞ self-similar blowup profile with unbounded stream function and infinite spatial blowup rate is constructed; for α<1/3, smooth profiles with bounded stream function and finite spatial blowup rate are obtained.
en.help_to_user: Provides insight into the mathematical construction and regularity conditions for self-similar blowup in the 3D Euler equations.

idea_spark.idea: Combine numerical approximate profiles with analytic perturbation to construct exact self-similar solutions.
idea_spark.risk: The accuracy and convergence of the numerical approximate profile require rigorous justification, and extension to higher dimensions may face technical challenges.
idea_spark.inspiration: Starting from a 1D model and gradually increasing dimension offers a modular approach to constructing singularities in complex PDEs.

papers.title: Fast contracted Clebsch--Gordan tensor products for equivariant graph neural networks
papers.summary: We present an $\mathcal{O}(L^3)$ algorithm for evaluating contracted Clebsch--Gordan tensor products in $\mathrm{O}(3)$-equivariant machine learning potentials at fixed Canonical Polyadic (CP) rank. Mapping the angular integral to a structured Gauss--Legendre and Fourier tensor-product grid decouples the radial channel contractions from the angular transforms. The antisymmetric parity-odd Clebsch--Gordan channels, unreachable by the symmetric pointwise product on a scalar $S^2$ grid, are recovered through the surface-curl pairing $\hat r \cdot [\nabla_{S^2} A \times \nabla_{S^2} B]$, the spherical Poisson bracket, which supplies the $L=1$ angular momentum on the grid while preserving rotational equivariance. The construction extends to parity-aware equivariant message passing in atomic-cluster-expansion-style architectures and is verified by direct numerical quadrature. The full uncontracted Clebsch--Gordan tensor product remains subject to the $\mathcal{O}(L^4)$ output-size lower bound. A benchmark shows wall-clock scaling empirically as $L^2$ across the practical $l_{\max}$ range. For the on-site contraction this is pre-asymptotic, giving way to $L^3$ at large $l_{\max}$. For message passing it is structural and the runtime is memory-bandwidth bound on $L^2$-sized grid tensors.

ai.tldr: 提出一种O(L^3)算法，在固定CP秩下快速计算O(3)等变图神经网络中的收缩Clebsch-Gordan张量积。
ai.motivation: 现有等变图神经网络中Clebsch-Gordan张量积计算复杂度高，限制了高角动量截断的应用。
ai.method: 将角度积分映射到结构化Gauss-Legendre和Fourier张量积网格上，解耦径向收缩与角度变换；通过球面泊松括号恢复奇宇称通道。
ai.result: 算法在实用l_max范围内经验缩放为L^2，消息传递部分受内存带宽限制，整体复杂度为O(L^3)。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种高效实现等变消息传递的方法，适用于原子团簇展开等架构。

idea_spark.idea: 利用球面泊松括号在网格上高效计算奇宇称Clebsch-Gordan通道。
idea_spark.risk: CP秩固定可能限制模型表达能力，高l_max时预渐近行为可能影响实际性能。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将连续角度积分离散化为结构化网格，借鉴了谱方法的思路。

en.tldr: We present an O(L^3) algorithm for fast contracted Clebsch-Gordan tensor products in O(3)-equivariant graph neural networks at fixed CP rank.
en.motivation: Existing Clebsch-Gordan tensor product computations in equivariant GNNs are expensive, limiting high angular momentum cutoffs.
en.method: Mapping the angular integral to a structured Gauss-Legendre and Fourier tensor-product grid decouples radial contractions from angular transforms; parity-odd channels are recovered via the spherical Poisson bracket.
en.result: The algorithm empirically scales as L^2 in practical l_max ranges; message passing is memory-bandwidth bound on L^2-sized grid tensors, with overall O(L^3) complexity.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient implementation of equivariant message passing for architectures like atomic cluster expansion.

idea_spark.idea: Use the spherical Poisson bracket to efficiently compute parity-odd Clebsch-Gordan channels on a grid.
idea_spark.risk: Fixed CP rank may limit model expressivity; pre-asymptotic behavior at high l_max could affect practical performance.
idea_spark.inspiration: Discretizing continuous angular integrals onto structured grids draws from spectral methods.

papers.title: Asymptotically Self-Similar Blowup for 3D Incompressible Euler with $C^{1, 1/3-}$ Velocity II: 3D Profiles, Blowup, and Limiting behavior
papers.summary: For any $α\in (0, 1/3)$, we construct exact $C^{1,α}$ self-similar blowup profiles for the 3D incompressible Euler equation without swirl, and build on them to prove asymptotically self-similar blowup from $C_c^α$ initial vorticity and $C^{1,α}\cap L^2$ initial velocity. Moreover, we provide a complete characterization of the limiting behavior of the $C^{1,α}$ blowup profiles and the associated $C^{1,α}$ blowup solutions as $α\to(1/3)^-$. Specifically, as $α\to(1/3)^-$, the spatial blowup rate $c_{x,α}$ diverges to $\infty$, while the $C^{1,α}$ blowup profile $Ω_{*,α}^θ$ asymptotically factorizes and converges strongly in a weighted $L^\infty$ norm to a nonzero constant multiple of $r^{1/3} \bar W_{1/3 }(z)$, where $ \bar W_{1/3}$ is a $C^\infty$ 1D blowup profile. Our construction is inspired by the Hou--Zhang blowup scenario. Using a fixed-point argument, we lift the $C^\infty$ blowup profiles for a 1D model constructed in the companion work [11] to exact 3D blowup profiles. To overcome the lack of $r$-directional decay in the approximate profile and capture the anisotropic structure, we develop a family of anisotropic weighted estimates and introduce a crucial integration-by-parts method along trajectories that exploits the equation twice. We then develop a finite codimension stability argument in a low-regularity setting to prove stability of the 3D profiles and establish asymptotically self-similar blowup. This blowup result is sharp in view of the global regularity theory for axisymmetric Euler without swirl with $C^{1,1/3+}\cap L^2$ initial velocity. To the best of our knowledge, our results provide the first example in which a singularity from a 1D nonlocal fluid model is lifted to construct blowup for incompressible fluid equations in $\mathbb{R}^2$ or $\mathbb{R}^3$.

ai.tldr: 构造了3D不可压缩欧拉方程无旋轴对称情形下的C^{1,α}自相似爆破剖面，并证明了从C_c^α涡量和C^{1,α}∩L^2速度出发的渐近自相似爆破。
ai.motivation: 证明3D欧拉方程在低正则性下存在尖锐的爆破现象，并揭示α→(1/3)^-时剖面的极限行为。
ai.method: 利用不动点论证将1D模型的光滑爆破剖面提升为3D精确剖面；发展各向异性加权估计和沿轨道的分部积分方法；在低正则性下建立有限余维稳定性论证。
ai.result: 对任意α∈(0,1/3)构造了精确的C^{1,α}自相似爆破剖面，并证明了渐近自相似爆破；当α→(1/3)^-时，空间爆破率发散，剖面渐近分解为常数倍r^{1/3}乘以光滑1D剖面。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了3D欧拉方程爆破构造的完整框架，包括剖面构造、稳定性分析和极限行为刻画。

idea_spark.idea: 将1D非局部流体模型的爆破剖面提升为3D精确剖面，并利用有限余维稳定性论证实现渐近自相似爆破。
idea_spark.risk: 构造依赖于无旋轴对称假设，推广到一般3D情形可能面临额外困难。
idea_spark.inspiration: Hou–Zhang爆破场景为构造提供了关键启发，1D模型到3D的升维策略值得借鉴。

en.tldr: Construct exact C^{1,α} self-similar blowup profiles for 3D incompressible Euler without swirl and prove asymptotically self-similar blowup from C_c^α vorticity and C^{1,α}∩L^2 velocity.
en.motivation: To demonstrate sharp blowup for 3D Euler at low regularity and characterize the limiting behavior of profiles as α→(1/3)^-.
en.method: Lift C^∞ blowup profiles from a 1D model to exact 3D profiles via a fixed-point argument; develop anisotropic weighted estimates and an integration-by-parts method along trajectories; establish finite codimension stability in a low-regularity setting.
en.result: For any α∈(0,1/3), construct exact C^{1,α} self-similar blowup profiles and prove asymptotically self-similar blowup; as α→(1/3)^-, the spatial blowup rate diverges and the profile factorizes to a constant multiple of r^{1/3} times a smooth 1D profile.
en.help_to_user: Provides a complete framework for constructing blowup in 3D Euler, including profile construction, stability analysis, and limiting behavior.

idea_spark.idea: Lift 1D nonlocal fluid model blowup profiles to 3D exact profiles and achieve asymptotically self-similar blowup via finite codimension stability.
idea_spark.risk: Construction relies on axisymmetry without swirl; generalization to full 3D may face additional challenges.
idea_spark.inspiration: The Hou–Zhang blowup scenario provides key inspiration; the 1D-to-3D lifting strategy is worth adopting.

papers.title: Uncovering the Representation Geometry of Minimal Cores in Overcomplete Reasoning Traces
papers.summary: Language models often generate long chain-of-thought traces, but it remains unclear how much of this reasoning is necessary for preserving the final prediction. We study this through the lens of overcomplete reasoning traces: generated traces that contain more intermediate steps than are needed to support the model's answer. We define the minimal core as the smallest subset of steps that preserves either the final answer or predictive distribution, and introduce metrics for compression ratio, redundancy mass, step necessity, and necessity concentration. Across six deliberative reasoning benchmarks spanning arithmetic, competition mathematics, expert scientific reasoning, and commonsense multi-hop QA, we find substantial overcompleteness: on average, 46% of steps are removable under greedy minimal-core extraction while preserving the original answer in 86% of cases. We also find that predictive support is concentrated: the top three steps account for 65% of measured necessity mass on average. Beyond compression, minimal cores expose a cleaner geometry of reasoning: compared with full traces, they improve correct-incorrect trace separation by 11 points, reduce estimated intrinsic dimensionality by 34%, and transfer across model families with 85% off-diagonal answer retention. Theoretically, we establish existence of minimal sufficient subsets, local irreducibility guarantees for greedy elimination, and certificates of overcompleteness and sparse necessity. Together, these results suggest that full reasoning traces are often verbose and overcomplete, while minimal cores isolate the effective support underlying language-model predictions.

ai.tldr: 语言模型生成的推理轨迹常包含冗余步骤，本文定义最小核心为保留最终答案的最小步骤子集，发现平均46%的步骤可移除，且最小核心能改善推理几何结构。
ai.motivation: 探究语言模型长链式推理轨迹中哪些步骤是真正必要的，以及冗余步骤对预测的影响。
ai.method: 定义最小核心、压缩比、冗余质量、步骤必要性和必要性集中度等指标，在六个推理基准上通过贪心消除提取最小核心。
ai.result: 平均46%的步骤可移除，86%情况下保留原答案；前三个步骤集中65%的必要性质量；最小核心提升正误轨迹分离11%，降低内在维度34%，跨模型保留85%答案。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解语言模型推理中的冗余程度，提供压缩推理轨迹的方法，并揭示更清晰的推理几何结构。

idea_spark.idea: 利用最小核心进行推理轨迹压缩，在保持预测质量的同时减少计算开销。
idea_spark.risk: 贪心消除可能遗漏全局最优子集，且最小核心的泛化性需在更多模型和任务上验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受稀疏表示和最小充分统计量启发，将推理步骤视为高维表示并寻找其低维支撑。
