en.tldr: Proposes OPTIMUS, a framework using dynamic programming and mixed-integer programming to optimize retail space allocation, achieving sales and margin lifts.
en.motivation: Limited retail bay capacity must be allocated among planograms under business and operational constraints to maximize sales and profit.
en.method: Formulates the problem as a linear binary knapsack model, solves assortment decisions exactly with dynamic programming, then integrates with a second-stage bay optimization mixed-integer program.
en.result: Across ten optimization runs over multiple departments and store clusters, average sales lift of 11.8% and average margin lift of 9.5%.
en.help_to_user: Helps retail managers make interpretable, reproducible shelf-space allocation decisions to improve overall performance.

idea_spark.risk: DP time complexity O(nc) may become a bottleneck with extremely large SKU counts; approximation or parallelization may be needed.
idea_spark.inspiration: Retail space optimization can leverage classic OR models but must incorporate real-world constraints like assortment similarity for practical deployment.

papers.title: Documentation-Guided Agentic Codebase Migration from C to Rust
papers.summary: Migrating legacy C repositories to Rust promises stronger memory safety, but existing translators often work at the level of files or functions and miss architectural intent. We present RustPrint, a documentation-guided agentic framework for repository-level C-to-Rust migration. RustPrint first converts the source repository into architecture-aware documentation and treats it as a migration blueprint capturing module structure, data flow, APIs, and design rationale. Coding agents then use this blueprint to plan crates, implement modules, check compilability, reduce unsafe code, and iteratively refine the translated repository. RustPrint next compares documentation from the Rust output against the source documentation and uses mismatches as repair signals. It also translates and runs source test suites so runtime failures can guide targeted fixes. Experiments on eight real-world C repositories ranging from 11K to 84K LoC show that RustPrint compiles every target under both an open-weight (Kimi-K2-Instruct) and a closed-weight (GPT-5.4) backbone, while prior LLM-based translators (Self-Repair, EvoC2Rust) fail repository-wide. With the open-weight Kimi-K2-Instruct backbone, RustPrint exceeds an agentic Claude Code baseline on feature preservation (93.26% vs. 52.52%) and on cross-evaluation test pass rate (95.17% vs. 79.85%). These results suggest that documentation-guided coordination is a useful direction for scalable codebase migration.

ai.tldr: 提出文档引导的智能体框架RustPrint，实现从C仓库到Rust的仓库级迁移，在八个真实仓库上编译成功且特征保持率超过93%。
ai.motivation: 现有C到Rust翻译器多基于文件或函数级别，忽略架构意图，导致迁移后代码难以维护且不安全代码多。
ai.method: 先转换源码为架构感知文档作为蓝图，编码智能体据此规划crate、实现模块、检查编译、减少unsafe代码并迭代优化；再对比输出文档与源文档的差异作为修复信号，同时翻译并运行测试套件以指导修复。
ai.result: 在八个11K-84K LoC的真实C仓库上，RustPrint在开源和闭源LLM骨干下均编译成功，特征保持率93.26%，交叉评估测试通过率95.17%，远超基线。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种可扩展的仓库级代码迁移方法，帮助开发者将遗留C代码安全高效地迁移到Rust。

idea_spark.idea: 将架构文档作为迁移蓝图，结合智能体迭代和测试反馈，实现仓库级代码转换。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖LLM生成文档和代码的质量，可能引入语义偏差或遗漏关键架构细节。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用文档差异作为修复信号，类似测试驱动开发，但以架构文档为规范。

en.tldr: Proposes RustPrint, a documentation-guided agentic framework for repository-level C-to-Rust migration, achieving compilation and 93.26% feature preservation on eight real-world repositories.
en.motivation: Existing C-to-Rust translators operate at file or function level, missing architectural intent, leading to unmaintainable code and excessive unsafe code.
en.method: Converts source repository into architecture-aware documentation as a blueprint; coding agents plan crates, implement modules, check compilability, reduce unsafe code, and iteratively refine; compares output documentation with source for repair signals; translates and runs test suites to guide fixes.

en.help_to_user: Provides a scalable repository-level code migration method to safely and efficiently port legacy C code to Rust.

idea_spark.idea: Use architecture documentation as a migration blueprint, combined with iterative agents and test feedback, for repository-level code transformation.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on LLM quality for documentation and code generation, potentially introducing semantic gaps or missing critical architectural details.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using documentation mismatches as repair signals, similar to test-driven development but with architectural documentation as specification.

papers.title: FedStain: Modeling Higher-Order Stain Statistics for Federated Domain Generalization in Computational Pathology
papers.summary: Robust whole-slide image (WSI) analysis under strict data-governance remains challenging due to substantial cross-institutional stain heterogeneity. Domain generalization (DG) mitigates these shifts but typically requires centralized data, conflicting with privacy regulations. Federated learning (FedL) provides a decentralized alternative; however, existing FedL and federated DG (FedDG) approaches rely almost exclusively on low-order statistics, assuming Gaussian-like stain distributions. In contrast, real-world staining processes often produce asymmetric, heavy-tailed color distributions due to biochemical diffusion and scanner nonlinearity. Consequently, current methods fail to model the higher-order, non-Gaussian characteristics dominating real-world stain variability. To address this, we propose FedStain, a stain-aware FedDG framework explicitly incorporating higher-order stain moments--skewness and kurtosis--as compact statistical descriptors exchanged during federated optimization. These descriptors require no pixel-level data transmission, preserving strict privacy and communication efficiency, while enabling the global model to capture stain variability missed by low-order statistics. FedStain also employs a contrastive, cross-site parameter aggregation strategy to promote stain-invariant representations without relaxing data constraints. Extensive experiments on Camelyon17 and our new MvMidog-Fed benchmark show FedStain yields consistent improvements, outperforming state-of-the-art FedL, DG, and FedDG baselines by up to +3.9% absolute accuracy. To our knowledge, FedStain is the first FedDG approach to explicitly model higher-order stain statistics, enabling robust cross-institutional deployment in computational pathology.

ai.tldr: 提出FedStain，首个在联邦域泛化中显式建模高阶染色统计量（偏度和峰度）的方法，无需传输像素级数据，在计算病理学跨机构部署中提升鲁棒性。
ai.motivation: 现有联邦学习和联邦域泛化方法仅依赖低阶统计量（假设高斯分布），无法捕捉真实染色过程中非对称、重尾的色分布，导致跨机构泛化能力不足。
ai.method: 在联邦优化中交换紧凑的高阶染色矩（偏度和峰度）作为统计描述符，并采用对比式跨站点参数聚合策略，促进染色不变表示。
ai.result: 在Camelyon17和新MvMidog-Fed基准上，FedStain一致优于SOTA联邦学习、域泛化和联邦域泛化基线，绝对准确率提升最高达+3.9%。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何在不共享像素数据的前提下，利用高阶统计量提升病理图像分析的跨机构泛化能力。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将高阶统计量与生成模型结合，在联邦框架下合成多样化的染色风格数据，进一步增强泛化。
idea_spark.risk: 高阶统计量可能对极端异常值敏感，需设计鲁棒的聚合机制；另外，偏度和峰度的计算依赖足够样本量，小站点可能估计不准。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从染色过程的生化扩散和扫描仪非线性出发，启发我们关注数据生成机制中的非高斯特性，而非仅依赖常见的一阶、二阶统计量。

en.tldr: Proposes FedStain, the first method to explicitly model higher-order stain statistics (skewness and kurtosis) for federated domain generalization in computational pathology, without transmitting pixel-level data, improving robustness across institutions.
en.motivation: Existing federated learning and federated domain generalization methods rely solely on low-order statistics (assuming Gaussian distributions), failing to capture the asymmetric, heavy-tailed color distributions of real-world staining, limiting cross-institution generalization.
en.method: Exchanges compact higher-order stain moments (skewness and kurtosis) as statistical descriptors during federated optimization, and employs a contrastive cross-site parameter aggregation strategy to promote stain-invariant representations.
en.result: On Camelyon17 and the new MvMidog-Fed benchmark, FedStain consistently outperforms state-of-the-art federated learning, domain generalization, and federated domain generalization baselines, achieving up to +3.9% absolute accuracy improvement.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to leverage higher-order statistics to improve cross-institution generalization of pathological image analysis without sharing pixel data.

idea_spark.idea: Explore combining higher-order statistics with generative models to synthesize diverse stain styles under the federated framework, further enhancing generalization.
idea_spark.risk: Higher-order statistics may be sensitive to extreme outliers, requiring robust aggregation mechanisms; also, skewness and kurtosis estimation needs sufficient samples, which may be unreliable for small sites.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the biochemical diffusion and scanner nonlinearity in staining processes, highlighting the importance of modeling non-Gaussian characteristics beyond common first- and second-order statistics.

papers.title: KGPFN: Unlocking the Potential of Knowledge Graph Foundation Model via In-Context Learning
papers.summary: Knowledge graph (KG) foundation models aim to generalize across graphs with unseen entities and relations by learning transferable relational structure. However, most existing methods primarily emphasize relation-level universality, while in-context learning, the other pillar of foundation models remains under-explored for KG reasoning. In KGs, context is inherently structured and heterogeneous: effective prediction requires conditioning on the local context around the query entities as well as the global context that summarizes how a relation behaves across many instances. We propose KGPFN, a KG foundation model using Prior-data Fitted Network that unifies transferable relational regularities with inference-time in-context learning from structured context. KGPFN first learns relation representations via message passing on relation graphs to capture cross-graph relational invariances. For query-specific reasoning, it encodes local neighborhoods using a multi-layer NBFNet as local context. To enable ICL at global scale, it constructs relation-specific global context by retrieving a large set of instances of the query relation together with their local neighborhoods, and aggregates them within a Prior-Data Fitted Network framework that combines feature-level and sample-level attention. Through multi-graph pretraining on diverse KGs, KGPFN learns when to instantiate reusable patterns and when to override them using contextual evidence. Experiments on 57 KG benchmarks demonstrate that KGPFN achieves strong adaptation to previously unseen graphs through in-context learning alone, consistently outperforming competitive fine-tuned KG foundation models. Our code is available at https://github.com/HKUST-KnowComp/KGPFN.

    "method": "Learns relation representations via message passing on relation graphs, encodes local neighborhoods with multi-layer NBFNet, and retrieves a large set of instances of the query relation with their local neighborhoods to construct global context, aggregated via feature-level and sample-level attention within a Prior-Data Fitted Network.",
    "result": "On 57 KG benchmarks, achieves strong adaptation to unseen graphs through in-context learning alone, consistently outperforming competitive fine-tuned KG foundation models.",
    "help_to_user": "Helps users understand how to incorporate in-context learning into KG foundation models for zero-fine-tuning cross-graph reasoning.",
    "idea_spark": {
      "transferable": "The combination of Prior-Data Fitted Network with structured context retrieval can be transferred to reasoning tasks on other graph-structured data (e.g., social networks, molecular graphs).",
      "idea": "Explore sample-level attention over global context instances in KG reasoning to dynamically decide when to reuse transferable patterns and when to override them with contextual evidence.",
      "risk": "Global context retrieval may introduce noise or redundant instances, affecting inference efficiency and accuracy; computational cost of large-scale retrieval needs optimization.",
      "inspiration": "Inspired by in-context learning in large language models, uses structured context (local neighborhoods + global relation instances) as prompts for the foundation model to adapt at inference time."
  }
}

papers.title: SteerSeg: Attention Steering for Reasoning Video Segmentation
papers.summary: Video reasoning segmentation requires localizing objects across video frames from natural language expressions, often involving spatial reasoning and implicit references. Recent approaches leverage frozen large vision-language models (LVLMs) by extracting attention maps and using them as spatial priors for segmentation, enabling training-free grounding. However, these attention maps are optimized for text generation rather than spatial localization, often resulting in diffuse and ambiguous grounding signals. In this work, we introduce SteerSeg, a lightweight framework that identifies attention misalignment as the key bottleneck in attention-based grounding and proposes to steer attention at its source through input-level conditioning. SteerSeg combines learnable soft prompts with reasoning-guided Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting. The soft prompts reshape the attention distribution to produce more spatially concentrated maps, while CoT-derived attributes resolve ambiguity among similar objects by guiding attention toward the correct instance. The resulting attention maps are converted into point prompts across keyframes to guide a segmentation model, while candidate tracklets are ranked and selected using correlation-based scoring. Our approach freezes the LVLM and segmentation model parameters and learns only a small set of soft prompts, preserving the model's pretrained reasoning capabilities while significantly improving grounding. Despite being trained only on Ref-YouTube-VOS, SteerSeg generalizes well across diverse benchmarks, significantly improving the spatial grounding capability of LVLMs. Project page: https://steerseg.github.io

ai.tldr: 提出SteerSeg框架，通过输入级注意力引导和软提示，在不微调大视觉语言模型的情况下提升视频推理分割的空间定位能力。
ai.motivation: 现有方法利用冻结LVLM的注意力图作为空间先验，但注意力图针对文本生成优化，导致定位信号模糊。
ai.method: 结合可学习的软提示和推理引导的链式思维提示，重塑注意力分布并消除歧义；将注意力图转换为点提示，通过相关性评分选择候选轨迹。
ai.result: 仅在Ref-YouTube-VOS上训练，即可在多个基准上泛化，显著提升LVLM的空间定位能力。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种轻量级、无需微调大模型即可提升视频分割中空间推理准确性的方法。

idea_spark.idea: 利用链式思维推理生成属性描述，辅助注意力图聚焦正确目标实例。
idea_spark.risk: 软提示可能过拟合训练数据分布，导致泛化到新场景时效果下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从注意力错位角度出发，通过输入级干预而非模型修改来优化定位，思路简洁有效。

en.tldr: Proposes SteerSeg, a lightweight framework that improves spatial grounding for video reasoning segmentation by steering attention at the input level via learnable soft prompts and chain-of-thought prompting, without fine-tuning the large vision-language model.
en.motivation: Existing methods use attention maps from frozen LVLMs as spatial priors, but these maps are optimized for text generation, leading to diffuse and ambiguous grounding signals.
en.method: Combines learnable soft prompts with reasoning-guided chain-of-thought prompting to reshape attention distribution and resolve ambiguity; converts attention maps into point prompts and selects candidate tracklets via correlation scoring.
en.result: Trained only on Ref-YouTube-VOS, it generalizes across diverse benchmarks, significantly improving the spatial grounding capability of LVLMs.
en.help_to_user: Provides a lightweight, fine-tuning-free approach to enhance spatial reasoning accuracy in video segmentation tasks.

idea_spark.idea: Leverages chain-of-thought reasoning to generate attribute descriptions that help attention maps focus on the correct target instance.
idea_spark.risk: Soft prompts may overfit to the training data distribution, potentially degrading performance on novel scenarios.
idea_spark.inspiration: Addresses attention misalignment as the bottleneck and intervenes at the input level rather than modifying the model, offering a simple yet effective solution.

papers.title: HASTE: Training-Free Video Diffusion Acceleration via Head-Wise Adaptive Sparse Attention
papers.summary: Diffusion-based video generation has advanced substantially in visual fidelity and temporal coherence, but practical deployment remains limited by the quadratic complexity of full attention. Training-free sparse attention is attractive because it accelerates pretrained models without retraining, yet existing online top-$p$ sparse attention still spends non-negligible cost on mask prediction and applies shared thresholds despite strong head-level heterogeneity. We show that these two overlooked factors limit the practical speed-quality trade-off of training-free sparse attention in Video DiTs. To address them, we introduce a head-wise adaptive framework with two plug-in components: Temporal Mask Reuse, which skips unnecessary mask prediction based on query-key drift, and Error-guided Budgeted Calibration, which assigns per-head top-$p$ thresholds by minimizing measured model-output error under a global sparsity budget. On Wan2.1-1.3B and Wan2.1-14B, our method consistently improves XAttention and SVG2, achieving up to 1.93 times speedup at 720P while maintaining competitive video quality and similarity metrics.

ai.tldr: 提出一种无需训练的头自适应稀疏注意力框架，通过时间掩码复用和误差引导预算校准，在保持视频质量的同时实现最高1.93倍加速。
ai.motivation: 现有训练无关稀疏注意力在视频扩散模型中因掩码预测开销和忽略头异质性而限制了速度-质量权衡。
ai.method: 包含两个即插即用组件：时间掩码复用（根据查询-键漂移跳过不必要的掩码预测）和误差引导预算校准（在全局稀疏预算下最小化模型输出误差来分配各头的top-p阈值）。
ai.result: 在Wan2.1-1.3B和Wan2.1-14B上，一致优于XAttention和SVG2，720P下实现最高1.93倍加速，同时保持有竞争力的视频质量和相似度指标。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何在不重新训练的情况下加速视频扩散模型，并提供了具体的加速方法和效果数据。

idea_spark.idea: 将误差引导的预算校准与时间掩码复用结合，实现动态稀疏注意力调度。
idea_spark.risk: 误差引导校准可能引入额外计算开销，且对全局稀疏预算的敏感度需要仔细调参。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用模型输出误差作为指导信号来分配注意力预算，启发其他资源分配问题的自适应优化方法。

idea_spark.idea: 将误差引导的预算校准与时间掩码复用结合，实现动态稀疏注意力调度。
idea_spark.risk: 误差引导校准可能引入额外计算开销，且对全局稀疏预算的敏感度需要仔细调参。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用模型输出误差作为指导信号来分配注意力预算，启发其他资源分配问题的自适应优化方法。

en.tldr: Proposes a training-free head-wise adaptive sparse attention framework that achieves up to 1.93× speedup at 720P while maintaining competitive video quality via Temporal Mask Reuse and Error-guided Budgeted Calibration.
en.motivation: Existing training-free sparse attention in video diffusion models is limited by mask prediction overhead and neglect of head-level heterogeneity, degrading the speed-quality trade-off.
en.method: Two plug-in components: Temporal Mask Reuse (skips unnecessary mask prediction based on query-key drift) and Error-guided Budgeted Calibration (assigns per-head top-p thresholds by minimizing model-output error under a global sparsity budget).
en.result: On Wan2.1-1.3B and Wan2.1-14B, consistently outperforms XAttention and SVG2, achieving up to 1.93× speedup at 720P with competitive video quality and similarity metrics.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to accelerate video diffusion models without retraining, providing specific acceleration methods and performance data.

idea_spark.idea: Combine error-guided budget calibration with temporal mask reuse to enable dynamic sparse attention scheduling.
idea_spark.risk: Error-guided calibration may introduce additional computational overhead, and sensitivity to the global sparsity budget requires careful tuning.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using model-output error as a guiding signal to allocate attention budgets inspires adaptive optimization methods for other resource allocation problems.

papers.title: Discrimination Is Generation: Unifying Ranking and Retrieval from a Tokenizer Perspective
papers.summary: Semantic IDs (SIDs) define the generation space of generative recommendation and directly determine its personalization ceiling. However, existing tokenizers are trained independently with retrieval objectives, leaving personalization signals fully decoupled from the SID construction process -- a fundamental gap that causes generative retrieval to persistently lag behind discriminative ranking. In this paper, we rethink the essence of SIDs: \emph{ranking seeks argmax in item space while retrieval seeks argmax in token space; both are the same problem solved at different granularities.} Based on this insight, we propose \DIG (\textbf{D}iscrimination \textbf{I}s \textbf{G}eneration), which embeds the tokenizer inside a discriminative ranking model for end-to-end training -- the ranker naturally becomes a retrieval model, yielding two models from a single training run. \DIG is organized around a \emph{feature assignment taxonomy}: item-intrinsic static features are encoded into SIDs, user-item cross features (u2i) implicitly drive codebook boundaries toward recommendation decision boundaries during training, and an MLP$_\mathrm{u2t}$ distillation module approximates u2i at the token level for inference. Experiments on three public benchmarks and two industrial datasets demonstrate that \DIG simultaneously improves ranking, retrieval, and unified retrieval-ranking quality.

ai.tldr: 本文提出DIG方法，将分词器嵌入判别式排序模型中进行端到端训练，统一了生成式检索和判别式排序，同时提升排序、检索及统一检索-排序质量。
ai.motivation: 现有生成式推荐中语义ID的构建与检索目标独立训练，导致个性化信号与ID构建脱节，生成式检索性能落后于判别式排序。
ai.method: 提出DIG框架，基于“排序在物品空间求argmax，检索在token空间求argmax”的洞察，将分词器嵌入判别式排序模型联合训练；通过特征分配分类法（物品固有静态特征编码为SID，用户-物品交叉特征隐式驱动码本边界，MLP_u2t蒸馏模块在推理时近似交叉特征）实现统一。
ai.result: 在三个公开基准和两个工业数据集上，DIG同时提升了排序、检索及统一检索-排序的质量。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助推荐系统研究者理解如何通过端到端训练统一生成式检索与判别式排序，提升推荐性能。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将用户-物品交叉特征更直接地融入语义ID的构建过程，例如通过注意力机制动态调整码本边界。
idea_spark.risk: 端到端训练可能增加计算开销，且对大规模工业系统需要优化训练效率。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从“排序与检索本质相同”的视角出发，启发其他领域（如自然语言处理中的生成与判别任务）的统一框架设计。

zh.tldr: 本文提出DIG方法，将分词器嵌入判别式排序模型中进行端到端训练，统一了生成式检索和判别式排序，同时提升排序、检索及统一检索-排序质量。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将用户-物品交叉特征更直接地融入语义ID的构建过程，例如通过注意力机制动态调整码本边界。
idea_spark.risk: 端到端训练可能增加计算开销，且对大规模工业系统需要优化训练效率。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从“排序与检索本质相同”的视角出发，启发其他领域（如自然语言处理中的生成与判别任务）的统一框架设计。

en.tldr: This paper proposes DIG, which embeds the tokenizer inside a discriminative ranking model for end-to-end training, unifying generative retrieval and discriminative ranking, and simultaneously improving ranking, retrieval, and unified retrieval-ranking quality.
en.motivation: Existing tokenizers for generative recommendation are trained independently with retrieval objectives, causing a gap between personalization signals and SID construction, making generative retrieval lag behind discriminative ranking.
en.method: DIG is proposed based on the insight that ranking seeks argmax in item space while retrieval seeks argmax in token space. It embeds the tokenizer inside a discriminative ranking model for joint training, using a feature assignment taxonomy: item-intrinsic static features are encoded into SIDs, user-item cross features implicitly drive codebook boundaries, and an MLP_u2t distillation module approximates cross features at inference.
en.result: On three public benchmarks and two industrial datasets, DIG simultaneously improves ranking, retrieval, and unified retrieval-ranking quality.
en.help_to_user: Helps recommendation system researchers understand how to unify generative retrieval and discriminative ranking via end-to-end training to improve recommendation performance.

idea_spark.idea: Explore more direct integration of user-item cross features into SID construction, e.g., via attention mechanisms to dynamically adjust codebook boundaries.
idea_spark.risk: End-to-end training may increase computational cost, requiring optimization for large-scale industrial systems.
idea_spark.inspiration: The perspective that ranking and retrieval are essentially the same problem inspires unified framework designs in other domains, such as generation and discrimination in NLP.

papers.title: LLM-Enabled Automated Algorithm Design for Multiuser Fluid Antenna Communications
papers.summary: Fluid antenna is a new reconfigurable antenna technology that can dynamically adjust the positions or ports of radiating elements and therefore provides a new degree of freedom for wireless communications. However, the associated port selection is a challenging large-scale combinatorial optimization problem and difficult to solve. Existing manually designed heuristic algorithms are not only labor-intensive, but cannot achieve satisfactory performance. In this paper, we propose a novel paradigm that leverages large language models (LLMs) for automated design of optimization algorithms for fluid antenna systems without manual hyperheuristic tuning. Specifically, we study the problem of maximizing the minimum signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) in the downlink to ensure fairness among users by optimizing port selection and beamforming. We investigate two LLM-enabled algorithm optimization strategies. The first is to optimize the crossover and mutation operations to enhance the performance of the well-known genetic algorithm and the second is to design AutoPort, a new heuristic from scratch by LLM, to solve the optimization problem. Simulation results verify that the proposed method can achieve near-optimal performance and significant improvement over the conventional genetic algorithm and the deep learning approach.

ai.tldr: 提出利用大语言模型自动设计流体天线系统的优化算法，无需人工超启发式调参。
ai.motivation: 流体天线的端口选择是大型组合优化问题，现有手工启发式算法性能差且耗时。
ai.method: 利用LLM优化遗传算法的交叉变异操作，或从零设计新启发式AutoPort，联合优化端口选择和波束赋形。
ai.result: 所提方法接近最优性能，显著优于传统遗传算法和深度学习方法。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何用LLM自动生成无线通信中的优化算法。

idea_spark.idea: 尝试用LLM生成多种启发式算子并动态组合，形成自适应优化框架。
idea_spark.risk: LLM生成的算法可能缺乏理论保证，需要大量仿真验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将LLM作为算法生成器，替代传统手工设计，开启自动化算法设计新范式。

zh.result: 所提方法接近最优性能，显著优于传统遗传算法和深度学习方法。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何用LLM自动生成无线通信中的优化算法。

idea_spark.idea: 尝试用LLM生成多种启发式算子并动态组合，形成自适应优化框架。
idea_spark.risk: LLM生成的算法可能缺乏理论保证，需要大量仿真验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将LLM作为算法生成器，替代传统手工设计，开启自动化算法设计新范式。

en.tldr: Proposes using large language models to automatically design optimization algorithms for fluid antenna systems without manual hyperheuristic tuning.
en.motivation: Port selection in fluid antennas is a large-scale combinatorial optimization problem where existing handcrafted heuristics are labor-intensive and perform poorly.
en.method: Leverages LLMs to optimize crossover and mutation operations of genetic algorithms or to design a new heuristic AutoPort from scratch, jointly optimizing port selection and beamforming.
en.result: The proposed method achieves near-optimal performance and significantly outperforms conventional genetic algorithms and deep learning approaches.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand how LLMs can automatically generate optimization algorithms for wireless communications.

idea_spark.idea: Try using LLMs to generate multiple heuristic operators and dynamically combine them into an adaptive optimization framework.
idea_spark.risk: LLM-generated algorithms may lack theoretical guarantees and require extensive simulation validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using LLMs as algorithm generators replaces traditional manual design, opening a new paradigm for automated algorithm design.

papers.title: Distribution-Aware Algorithm Design with LLM Agents
papers.summary: We study learning when the learned object is executable solver code rather than a predictor. In this setting, correctness is not enough: two solvers may both return valid solutions on the deployment distribution while differing substantially in runtime. Given samples from an unknown task distribution, the learner returns code evaluated on fresh instances by both solution quality and execution time. Our central abstraction is a \emph{solver hint}: reusable structure inferred from samples and compiled into specialized solver code. We prove that the empirically fastest sample-consistent solver from a fixed library generalizes in both correctness and runtime, and that statistically identifiable hints can be recovered and compiled from polynomially many samples.   Empirically, we instantiate the framework with LLM code agents on \(21\) structured combinatorial-optimization target distributions across seven problem classes. The synthesized solvers reach mean normalized quality \(0.971\), improve by \(+0.224\) over the average heuristic pool and by \(+0.098\) over the highest-quality heuristic, and are \(336.9\times\), \(342.8\times\), and \(16.1\times\) faster than the quality-best heuristic, Gurobi, and the selected time-limited exact backend, respectively. On released PACE 2025 Dominating Set private instances, the synthesized solver is valid on all \(100\) graphs and runs about two orders of magnitude faster than top competition solvers, with a moderate quality gap. Inspection shows that many gains come from changing the computational scale: replacing ambient exponential search or general-purpose optimization with compiled distribution-specific computation.

ai.tldr: 研究如何利用LLM代理从任务分布样本中学习可执行的求解器代码，同时保证正确性和运行效率。
ai.motivation: 传统学习关注预测器，但许多实际场景需要可执行的求解器代码；仅正确性不够，运行时间也至关重要。
ai.method: 提出“求解器提示”抽象，从样本中推断可复用结构并编译为专用代码；理论证明样本一致求解器的泛化性，并给出多项式样本恢复条件。
ai.result: 在21个组合优化分布上，合成求解器平均质量0.971，比最优启发式高0.098，比Gurobi快342.8倍；在PACE 2025数据集上有效且快约两个数量级。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户自动生成针对特定分布的高效求解器代码，大幅减少手动调优和试错时间。

idea_spark.idea: 利用LLM从少量样本中自动发现并编译分布特有的计算捷径，替代通用指数搜索。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖样本代表性，若分布偏移过大可能导致生成代码失效或变慢。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将算法设计视为从数据中学习可执行代码，结合理论泛化保证与LLM的代码生成能力。

en.tldr: Studies learning executable solver code from task distribution samples using LLM agents, ensuring both correctness and runtime efficiency.
en.motivation: Traditional learning focuses on predictors, but many applications require executable solvers; correctness alone is insufficient—runtime matters.
en.method: Introduces 'solver hint' abstraction: reusable structure inferred from samples and compiled into specialized code; proves generalization of sample-consistent solvers and polynomial sample recovery.
en.result: On 21 combinatorial optimization distributions, synthesized solvers achieve mean quality 0.971, outperform best heuristic by 0.098, and are 342.8× faster than Gurobi; on PACE 2025 data, valid on all 100 graphs and ~100× faster than top solvers.
en.help_to_user: Helps users automatically generate efficient, distribution-specific solver code, reducing manual tuning and trial-and-error.

idea_spark.idea: Use LLMs to automatically discover and compile distribution-specific computational shortcuts from few samples, replacing generic exponential search.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on sample representativeness; significant distribution shift may cause generated code to fail or slow down.
idea_spark.inspiration: View algorithm design as learning executable code from data, combining theoretical generalization guarantees with LLM code generation capabilities.

papers.title: IG-Diff: Complex Night Scene Restoration with Illumination-Guided Diffusion Model
papers.summary: In nighttime circumstances, it is challenging for individuals and machines to perceive their surroundings. While prevailing image restoration methods adeptly handle singular forms of degradation, they falter when confronted with intricate nocturnal scenes, such as the concurrent presence of weather and low-light conditions. Compounding this challenge, the lack of paired data that encapsulates the coexistence of low-light situations and other forms of degradation hinders the development of a comprehensive end-to-end solution. In this work, we contribute complex nighttime scene datasets that simulate both illumination degradation and other forms of deterioration. To address the complexity of night degradation, we propose an integration of an illumination-guided module embedded in the diffusion model to guide the illumination restoration process. Our model can preserve texture fidelity while contending with the adversities posed by various degradation in low-light scenarios.

ai.tldr: 提出一种光照引导的扩散模型，用于同时处理夜间场景中的低光照和天气等多种退化，并构建了相应的复杂夜间场景数据集。
ai.motivation: 现有图像恢复方法只能处理单一退化，无法应对夜间复杂场景（如低光照与天气退化共存），且缺乏配对数据。
ai.method: 在扩散模型中嵌入光照引导模块，利用该模块指导光照恢复过程，同时保持纹理保真度。
ai.result: 模型能够在低光照条件下应对多种退化，并保持纹理细节。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何利用扩散模型和光照引导机制解决夜间复杂场景的恢复问题。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将光照引导与多模态信息（如深度、语义）结合，进一步提升复杂场景恢复效果。
idea_spark.risk: 光照引导模块可能对极端光照变化敏感，导致恢复结果不稳定。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受扩散模型在图像生成中的成功启发，将其条件生成能力用于退化恢复，并引入显式光照先验。

en.tldr: Proposes an illumination-guided diffusion model for restoring complex nighttime scenes with both low-light and weather degradation, along with a paired dataset.
en.motivation: Existing methods handle single degradation types but fail in complex nighttime scenes with coexisting low-light and weather issues, and lack paired training data.
en.method: Embeds an illumination-guided module into a diffusion model to guide the illumination restoration while preserving texture fidelity.
en.result: The model handles multiple degradations under low-light conditions and maintains texture details.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how diffusion models with illumination guidance can address complex nighttime scene restoration.

idea_spark.idea: Explore combining illumination guidance with multimodal information (e.g., depth, semantics) to further improve complex scene restoration.
idea_spark.risk: The illumination module may be sensitive to extreme lighting variations, causing unstable restoration.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the success of diffusion models in image generation, we adapt their conditional generation capability for degradation restoration with explicit illumination priors.

papers.title: Nonlinear Multiphysics Modeling of Batch Digester Discharge Dynamics with Rheology-Driven Hydraulic Transport and Drainability Coupling
papers.summary: Batch digester blowdown operations exhibit highly nonlinear hydraulic transport dynamics due to evolving slurry consistency and rheological uncertainty. This work presents a nonlinear dynamic model and a robust Sliding Mode Control (SMC) strategy for discharge-flow regulation in industrial batch digester systems. The proposed framework incorporates non-Newtonian slurry rheology, consistency-dependent hydraulic resistance, drainability effects, channeling phenomena, and nonlinear hydraulic transport behavior.

ai.tldr: 提出了一种考虑非牛顿浆料流变性和排水耦合的间歇蒸煮器排料非线性动态模型及滑模控制策略。
ai.motivation: 间歇蒸煮器排料过程因浆料稠度变化和流变不确定性而呈现高度非线性水力传输动态，现有模型难以准确描述和控制。
ai.method: 建立了包含非牛顿浆料流变学、稠度相关水力阻力、排水效应、沟流现象和非线性水力传输行为的动态模型，并设计了鲁棒滑模控制器。
ai.result: 所提框架能够有效应对排料过程中的非线性动态和不确定性，实现稳定的流量调节。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解工业间歇蒸煮器排料过程的非线性动态建模与滑模控制方法。

idea_spark.idea: 将排水效应和沟流现象纳入模型，可启发更精细的浆料处理系统控制设计。
idea_spark.risk: 模型复杂度高，参数辨识困难，实际应用可能需要简化或自适应策略。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合流变测量与在线稠度估计，实现自适应滑模控制，提升鲁棒性。

en.tldr: A nonlinear dynamic model and sliding mode control strategy for batch digester discharge, incorporating non-Newtonian slurry rheology and drainability coupling.
en.motivation: Batch digester blowdown exhibits highly nonlinear hydraulic transport due to evolving slurry consistency and rheological uncertainty, challenging accurate modeling and control.
en.method: A dynamic model integrating non-Newtonian slurry rheology, consistency-dependent hydraulic resistance, drainability effects, channeling phenomena, and nonlinear hydraulic transport is developed, along with a robust sliding mode controller.
en.result: The proposed framework effectively handles nonlinear dynamics and uncertainties, achieving stable discharge flow regulation.
en.help_to_user: Provides insight into nonlinear dynamic modeling and sliding mode control for industrial batch digester discharge processes.

idea_spark.idea: Incorporating drainability and channeling effects into the model can inspire more refined control designs for slurry handling systems.
idea_spark.risk: High model complexity and parameter identification challenges may require simplification or adaptive strategies in practice.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combine rheological measurements with online consistency estimation to enable adaptive sliding mode control for enhanced robustness.

papers.title: How Sensitive Are Radiomic AI Models to Acquisition Parameters?
papers.summary: A main barrier for the deployment of AI radiomic systems in clinical routine is their drop in performance under heterogeneous multicentre acquisition protocols. This work presents a performance-oriented framework for quantifying scan parameter sensitivity of radiomic AI models, while identifying clinically significant parameter regions associated with improved cross-dataset robustness. We formulate a mixed-effects framework for quantifying the influence that clinically relevant acquisition parameters have on models performance, while accounting for subject-level random effects. We have applied our framework to lung cancer diagnosis in CT scans using two independent multicentre datasets (a public database and own-collected data) and several SoA architectures. To evaluate across-database reproducibility, CT parameters have been adjusted using the data collected and tested on the public set. The optimal configuration selected is the current of the X-ray tube >= 200 mA, spiral pitch <= 1.5, slice thickness <= 1.25 mm, which balances diagnostic quality with low radiation dose. These configuration push metrics from 0.79+-0.04 sensitivity, 0.47+-0.10 specificity in low quality scans to 0.90+-0.10 sensitivity, 0.79 +- 0.13 specificity in high quality ones.

ai.tldr: 提出一个面向性能的框架，量化CT采集参数对放射组学AI模型的影响，并识别出提高跨数据集鲁棒性的临床重要参数区间。
ai.motivation: 放射组学AI模型在临床部署时因多中心采集协议差异导致性能下降，需要量化扫描参数敏感度并找到稳健的参数配置。
ai.method: 构建混合效应模型，在考虑受试者随机效应的同时，量化临床相关采集参数对模型性能的影响，并在两个独立多中心CT肺癌数据集上验证。
ai.result: 最优参数配置为管电流≥200 mA、螺距≤1.5、层厚≤1.25 mm，将低质量扫描的灵敏度从0.79提升至0.90，特异性从0.47提升至0.79。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何选择CT扫描参数以提升放射组学AI模型的跨中心泛化能力。

idea_spark.idea: 结合主动学习或贝叶斯优化，自动搜索最优扫描参数区间，减少人工试错成本。
idea_spark.risk: 参数优化可能过度拟合特定数据集，需在更多样化的多中心数据上验证泛化性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将模型性能与物理参数直接关联，启发设计“参数感知”的鲁棒AI模型，而非仅依赖数据增强。

zh.tldr: 提出一个面向性能的框架，量化CT采集参数对放射组学AI模型的影响，并识别出提高跨数据集鲁棒性的临床重要参数区间。
zh.motivation: 放射组学AI模型在临床部署时因多中心采集协议差异导致性能下降，需要量化扫描参数敏感度并找到稳健的参数配置。

idea_spark.risk: 参数优化可能过度拟合特定数据集，需在更多样化的多中心数据上验证泛化性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将模型性能与物理参数直接关联，启发设计“参数感知”的鲁棒AI模型，而非仅依赖数据增强。

en.tldr: A performance-oriented framework quantifies the influence of CT acquisition parameters on radiomic AI models, identifying clinically significant parameter ranges that improve cross-dataset robustness.
en.motivation: Radiomic AI models suffer performance drops under heterogeneous multi-center acquisition protocols, necessitating quantification of scan parameter sensitivity and identification of robust configurations.
en.method: A mixed-effects framework accounts for subject-level random effects while quantifying the impact of clinically relevant acquisition parameters on model performance, validated on two independent multi-center CT lung cancer datasets.
en.result: Optimal parameters (tube current ≥200 mA, spiral pitch ≤1.5, slice thickness ≤1.25 mm) improve sensitivity from 0.79 to 0.90 and specificity from 0.47 to 0.79 compared to low-quality scans.
en.help_to_user: Helps users select CT scan parameters to enhance cross-center generalizability of radiomic AI models.

idea_spark.idea: Combine active learning or Bayesian optimization to automatically search for optimal scan parameter intervals, reducing manual trial-and-error.
idea_spark.risk: Parameter optimization may overfit to specific datasets; validation on more diverse multi-center data is needed.
idea_spark.inspiration: Directly linking model performance to physical parameters inspires the design of 'parameter-aware' robust AI models beyond data augmentation.

papers.title: CalibAnyView: Beyond Single-View Camera Calibration in the Wild
papers.summary: Camera calibration is a fundamental prerequisite for reliable geometric perception, yet classical approaches rely on controlled acquisition setups that are impractical for in-the-wild imagery. Recent learning-based methods have shown promising results for single-view calibration, but inherently neglect geometric consistency across multiple views. We introduce CalibAnyView, a unified formulation that supports an arbitrary number of input views ($N \geq 1$) by explicitly modeling cross-view geometric consistency. To facilitate this, we construct a large-scale multi-view video dataset covering diverse real-world scenarios, including multiple camera models, dynamic scenes, realistic motion trajectories, and heterogeneous lens distortions. Building on this dataset, we develop a multi-view transformer that predicts dense perspective fields, which are further integrated into a geometric optimization framework to jointly estimate camera intrinsics and gravity direction. Extensive experiments demonstrate that CalibAnyView consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods, achieves strong robustness under single-view settings, and further improves with multi-view inference, providing a reliable foundation for downstream tasks such as 3D reconstruction and robotic perception in the wild.

ai.tldr: 提出CalibAnyView，一种支持任意数量输入视图的统一相机标定方法，通过多视图几何一致性显式建模，在单视图和多视图场景下均优于现有方法。
ai.motivation: 传统相机标定依赖受控采集环境，不适用于野外图像；现有单视图学习方法忽略多视图几何一致性。
ai.method: 构建大规模多视图视频数据集，开发多视图Transformer预测稠密透视场，结合几何优化框架联合估计相机内参和重力方向。
ai.result: 在单视图和多视图设置下均超越现有方法，多视图推理进一步提升性能，为3D重建和机器人感知提供可靠基础。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户实现任意数量视图的鲁棒相机标定，无需专用标定板，适用于野外场景。

idea_spark.idea: 利用透视场作为中间表示，将多视图信息统一到优化框架中，可能启发其他需要多视图融合的视觉任务。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖大规模多视图数据集，数据收集和标注成本高；在极端动态或纹理缺失场景下可能失效。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将几何先验与学习表示结合，实现从单视图到多视图的平滑扩展，可借鉴于其他需要灵活输入数量的任务。

idea_spark.inspiration: 将几何先验与学习表示结合，实现从单视图到多视图的平滑扩展，可借鉴于其他需要灵活输入数量的任务。

en.tldr: Proposes CalibAnyView, a unified camera calibration method supporting any number of input views by explicitly modeling multi-view geometric consistency, outperforming state-of-the-art in both single-view and multi-view settings.
en.motivation: Traditional calibration requires controlled setups, unsuitable for in-the-wild imagery; existing learning-based methods neglect multi-view geometric consistency.
en.method: Constructs a large-scale multi-view video dataset, develops a multi-view transformer to predict dense perspective fields, and integrates them into a geometric optimization framework for joint estimation of camera intrinsics and gravity direction.
en.result: Consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods, with robustness in single-view settings and further improvement via multi-view inference, enabling reliable 3D reconstruction and robotic perception in the wild.
en.help_to_user: Enables robust camera calibration from arbitrary number of views without calibration targets, suitable for real-world scenes.

idea_spark.idea: Using perspective fields as intermediate representation to unify multi-view information in an optimization framework may inspire other vision tasks requiring multi-view fusion.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on large-scale multi-view datasets with high collection and annotation costs; may fail in extreme dynamic or texture-less scenes.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining geometric priors with learned representations for smooth extension from single to multiple views can be adapted to tasks needing flexible input counts.

papers.title: Multiple-Bases Belief Propagation List Decoding for Quantum LDPC Codes
papers.summary: In this paper, we propose a belief-propagation (BP)-based decoder, termed the Multiple-Bases Belief-Propagation List Decoder (MBBP-LD), for quantum low-density parity-check (QLDPC) codes. The key idea is to generate \emph{structured decoding diversity} by constructing multiple redundant parity-check representations via cycle-free subtree decompositions of the Tanner graph, and running BP decoding in parallel across these representations. This extends the classical Multiple-Bases Belief-Propagation (MBBP) framework to the quantum setting while preserving the linear-time complexity and efficiency of standard BP decoding, and avoids the need for super-linear post-processing.   Simulation results demonstrate that MBBP-LD improves upon existing BP-based decoders, including BP with ordered statistics decoding (BP-OSD) and belief propagation with guided decimation (BPGD) across several QLDPC codes, while requiring substantially fewer total BP iterations. For bivariate bicycle codes $[[144,12,12]]$ and $[[288,12,18]]$, MBBP-LD achieves up to $20\%$ reduction in error rate compared to BPGD and up to $30\%$ compared to BP-OSD in the low- and moderate-error regimes. For the larger B1 code $[[882, 24, 18 \leq d \leq 24]]$, MBBP-LD attains comparable or improved performance relative to BPGD while maintaining BP-like decoding latency under parallel implementation.

ai.tldr: 提出了一种基于置信传播的列表解码器MBBP-LD，用于量子LDPC码，通过多基表示生成结构化解码多样性，在保持线性复杂度的同时提升了纠错性能。
ai.motivation: 现有量子LDPC码的BP解码器性能有限，且后处理复杂度高，需要一种高效且性能更好的解码方案。
ai.method: 利用Tanner图的无环子树分解构造多个冗余奇偶校验表示，并行运行BP解码，生成结构化解码多样性，扩展经典MBBP框架到量子领域。
ai.result: 在多个QLDPC码上，MBBP-LD相比BP-OSD和BPGD降低了20%-30%的错误率，且总BP迭代次数更少，并行实现下保持BP级延迟。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解一种新的量子LDPC码解码方法，在性能和效率之间取得良好平衡。

idea_spark.idea: 利用图分解生成多个解码路径，通过并行投票或融合提升解码可靠性。
idea_spark.risk: 子树分解的质量可能影响多样性收益，且并行实现需要额外硬件资源。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从经典MBBP和列表解码中获得启发，将多样性引入量子解码领域。

en.tldr: Proposes a belief-propagation-based list decoder MBBP-LD for quantum LDPC codes, generating structured decoding diversity via multiple parity-check representations while maintaining linear-time complexity.
en.motivation: Existing BP decoders for QLDPC codes have limited performance and require high-complexity post-processing; a more efficient and effective decoder is needed.
en.method: Constructs multiple redundant parity-check representations through cycle-free subtree decompositions of the Tanner graph, runs BP decoding in parallel, extending the classical MBBP framework to the quantum setting.
en.result: MBBP-LD reduces error rate by 20%-30% compared to BP-OSD and BPGD on several QLDPC codes, with fewer total BP iterations and BP-like latency under parallel implementation.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand a new decoding method for quantum LDPC codes that balances performance and efficiency.

idea_spark.idea: Use graph decomposition to generate multiple decoding paths and improve reliability through parallel voting or fusion.
idea_spark.risk: Quality of subtree decomposition may affect diversity gain; parallel implementation requires additional hardware resources.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by classical MBBP and list decoding to introduce diversity into quantum decoding.

papers.title: SceneFunRI: Reasoning the Invisible for Task-Driven Functional Object Localization
papers.summary: In real-world scenes, target objects may reside in regions that are not visible. While humans can often infer the locations of occluded objects from context and commonsense knowledge, this capability remains a major challenge for vision-language models (VLMs). To address this gap, we introduce SceneFunRI, a benchmark for Reasoning the Invisible. Based on the SceneFun3D dataset, SceneFunRI formulates the task as a 2D spatial reasoning problem via a semi-automatic pipeline and comprises 855 instances. It requires models to infer the locations of invisible functional objects from task instructions and commonsense reasoning. The strongest baseline model (Gemini 3 Flash) only achieves an CAcc@75 of 15.20, an mIoU of 0.74, and a Dist of 28.65. We group our prompting analysis into three categories: Strong Instruction Prompting, Reasoning-based Prompting, and Spatial Process of Elimination (SPoE). These findings indicate that invisible-region reasoning remains an unstable capability in current VLMs, motivating future work on models that more tightly integrate task intent, commonsense priors, spatial grounding, and uncertainty-aware search.

ai.tldr: 提出SceneFunRI基准，评估VLM在真实场景中推理不可见功能物体位置的能力。
ai.motivation: 现有VLM难以像人类一样利用上下文和常识推断被遮挡物体的位置。
ai.method: 基于SceneFun3D构建半自动标注的2D空间推理基准，包含855个实例，要求模型从任务指令和常识推理中定位不可见物体。
ai.result: 最强基线Gemini 3 Flash的CAcc@75仅15.20，mIoU 0.74，Dist 28.65，表明不可见区域推理仍是VLM的不稳定能力。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究人员评估和提升VLM在遮挡场景下的空间推理与常识结合能力。

idea_spark.idea: 结合不确定性感知搜索与任务意图、常识先验、空间定位的紧密集成模型。
idea_spark.risk: 基准规模有限（855实例），可能无法覆盖所有遮挡场景的多样性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类利用上下文推断遮挡物体位置的启发，推动VLM向更鲁棒的常识空间推理发展。

en.tldr: Introduces SceneFunRI, a benchmark for evaluating VLMs' ability to reason about invisible functional object locations in real-world scenes.
en.motivation: Current VLMs struggle to infer occluded object locations from context and commonsense, a capability humans naturally possess.
en.method: Built upon SceneFun3D, a semi-automatically annotated 2D spatial reasoning benchmark with 855 instances, requiring models to locate invisible objects from task instructions and commonsense reasoning.
en.result: The strongest baseline (Gemini 3 Flash) achieves only 15.20 CAcc@75, 0.74 mIoU, and 28.65 Dist, indicating that invisible-region reasoning remains unstable in current VLMs.
en.help_to_user: Helps researchers evaluate and improve VLMs' spatial reasoning and commonsense integration in occluded scenarios.

idea_spark.idea: Develop models that tightly integrate task intent, commonsense priors, spatial grounding, and uncertainty-aware search.
idea_spark.risk: Limited benchmark size (855 instances) may not cover the full diversity of occlusion scenarios.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by humans' ability to infer occluded object locations from context, pushing VLMs toward more robust commonsense spatial reasoning.

papers.title: Protected operators in non-local defect CFTs from AdS
papers.summary: For a local quantum field theory in anti-de Sitter space with conformal boundary conditions but without dynamical gravity, the boundary theory is generically a non-local conformal field theory. Such theories can support conformal defects, but the standard local-CFT arguments based on a boundary stress tensor and conserved currents do not apply. We argue that, under general assumptions, displacement and tilt operators nevertheless exist and have protected quantum numbers. The mechanism is a Goldstone-type phenomenon in AdS: defect-induced symmetry breaking on the boundary is spontaneous from the viewpoint of the local bulk theory, whose Ward identities enforce the corresponding protected defect operators. We illustrate the mechanism in weakly coupled defect RG flows, long-range Landau--Ginzburg models, 4D Maxwell theory, and Yang--Mills theory in AdS.

ai.tldr: 本文论证了在反德西特空间中具有共形边界条件的非局域共形场论中，位移算子和倾斜算子存在且具有受保护的量子数，其机制类似于AdS中的戈德斯通现象。
ai.motivation: 标准局域CFT中基于边界应力张量和守恒流的论证不适用于非局域CFT，需要新的机制来理解缺陷算子的保护性质。
ai.method: 通过AdS中的戈德斯通型现象，利用局域体理论的Ward恒等式推导边界上缺陷算子的保护性质，并在弱耦合缺陷RG流、长程朗道-金兹堡模型、4D麦克斯韦理论和杨-米尔斯理论中举例说明。
ai.result: 位移和倾斜算子存在且具有受保护的量子数，缺陷诱导的对称性破缺在体理论视角下是自发破缺。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解非局域CFT中缺陷算子的基本性质及其与AdS/CFT对偶的关系。

idea_spark.idea: 探索非局域CFT中其他类型缺陷算子的保护性质，或利用该机制构造新的全息对偶。
idea_spark.risk: 非局域CFT的微扰计算可能复杂，且保护性质可能依赖于特定假设。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将体理论的对称性破缺与边界算子的保护性质联系起来，为研究非局域系统提供新视角。

en.tldr: This paper argues that in non-local conformal field theories with conformal boundary conditions in anti-de Sitter space, displacement and tilt operators exist and have protected quantum numbers, via a Goldstone-type mechanism in AdS.
en.motivation: Standard local CFT arguments based on boundary stress tensor and conserved currents do not apply to non-local CFTs, requiring a new mechanism to understand the protected nature of defect operators.
en.method: Using a Goldstone-type phenomenon in AdS, the Ward identities of the local bulk theory enforce protected defect operators on the boundary, illustrated in weakly coupled defect RG flows, long-range Landau-Ginzburg models, 4D Maxwell theory, and Yang-Mills theory in AdS.
en.result: Displacement and tilt operators exist with protected quantum numbers; defect-induced symmetry breaking is spontaneous from the bulk perspective.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand the basic properties of defect operators in non-local CFTs and their relation to the AdS/CFT correspondence.

idea_spark.idea: Explore the protected nature of other types of defect operators in non-local CFTs, or use this mechanism to construct new holographic duals.
idea_spark.risk: Perturbative calculations in non-local CFTs may be complex, and the protected nature may depend on specific assumptions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Connecting bulk symmetry breaking to boundary operator protection provides a new perspective for studying non-local systems.

papers.title: PVRF: All-in-one Adverse Weather Removal via Prior-modulated and Velocity-constrained Rectified Flow
papers.summary: Adverse weather removal (AWR) in real-world images remains challenging due to heterogeneous and unseen degradations, while distortion-driven training often yields overly smooth results. We propose PVRF, a unified framework that integrates zero-shot soft weather perceptions with velocity-constrained rectified-flow refinement. PVRF introduces an AWR-specific question answering module (AWR-QA) that uses frozen vision--language models (VLMs) to estimate soft probabilities of weather types and low-level attribute scores. These perceptions condition restoration networks via attribute-modulated normalization (AMN) and weather-weighted adapters (WWA), producing an anchor estimate for refinement. We then learn a terminal-consistent residual rectified flow with perception-adaptive source perturbation and a terminal-consistent velocity parameterization to stabilize learning near the terminal regime. Extensive experiments show that PVRF improves both fidelity and perceptual quality over state-of-the-art baselines, with strong cross-dataset generalization on single and combined degradations. Code will be released at https://github.com/dongw22/PVRF.

ai.tldr: 提出PVRF统一框架，结合零样本软天气感知与速度约束整流流，实现多种恶劣天气去除。
ai.motivation: 真实图像中天气退化复杂且未知，现有方法常产生过平滑结果。
ai.method: 利用冻结视觉语言模型估计天气类型与属性分数，通过属性调制归一化和天气加权适配器调节恢复网络，再学习终端一致的残差整流流。
ai.result: 在保真度和感知质量上超越现有基线，跨数据集泛化能力强。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种无需逐天气类型训练的统一去天气方法，适用于实际多变场景。

idea_spark.idea: 利用VLM的零样本能力为恢复网络提供先验，再通过流模型精细修正。
idea_spark.risk: VLM的感知可能不准确，导致错误条件化；流模型训练稳定性需验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将语言模型的语义理解与生成模型的物理约束结合，提升退化去除的鲁棒性。

idea_spark.inspiration: 将语言模型的语义理解与生成模型的物理约束结合，提升退化去除的鲁棒性。

en.tldr: PVRF is a unified framework integrating zero-shot soft weather perception with velocity-constrained rectified flow for adverse weather removal.
en.motivation: Real-world weather degradations are heterogeneous and unseen; existing methods often produce overly smooth results.
en.method: Uses frozen VLMs to estimate soft weather probabilities and attribute scores, conditions restoration networks via AMN and WWA, then learns a terminal-consistent residual rectified flow.
en.result: Outperforms state-of-the-art baselines in fidelity and perceptual quality with strong cross-dataset generalization.
en.help_to_user: Provides a single model that handles multiple weather types without per-type training, suitable for real-world variability.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage zero-shot VLM capabilities to provide priors for restoration networks, then refine with a flow model.
idea_spark.risk: VLM perceptions may be inaccurate, leading to incorrect conditioning; flow training stability needs verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combine semantic understanding from language models with physical constraints of generative models for robust degradation removal.

papers.title: Liouville theorem for immersed minimal surfaces in any codimension
papers.summary: For a proper immersed minimal disk in $\bf{R}^N$ with quadratic area growth, we show that any harmonic function whose negative part grows at a slow sub-linear rate is constant. This leads to a higher codimensional Bernstein theorem for minimal disks contained in a sub-linearly growing cone. The catenoid, helicoid and Enneper's family of surfaces together show that this result is optimal. We also show uniform Hölder regularity of harmonic functions.

ai.tldr: 证明了在二次面积增长条件下，浸入极小圆盘上的次线性增长调和函数必为常数，并得到高余维Bernstein定理。
ai.motivation: 将经典Liouville定理推广到高余维极小曲面，并探索最优条件。
ai.method: 利用调和函数的次线性增长假设与极小曲面的面积增长条件，结合正则性分析。
ai.result: 建立了高余维Bernstein定理，并通过例子证明结果最优。
ai.help_to_user: 为高余维极小曲面理论提供关键分析工具。

idea_spark.idea: 利用面积增长控制调和函数增长，可能用于研究非极小曲面上的函数性质。
idea_spark.risk: 高余维情形下几何复杂性增加，推广需谨慎。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合调和函数与面积增长，启发其他几何分析问题。

en.tldr: Proves that any harmonic function with sub-linear growth on a proper immersed minimal disk with quadratic area growth is constant, leading to a higher-codimension Bernstein theorem.
en.motivation: Extend the classical Liouville theorem to minimal surfaces in higher codimension and find optimal conditions.
en.method: Uses sub-linear growth of harmonic functions combined with area growth of minimal surfaces and regularity analysis.
en.result: Establishes a higher-codimension Bernstein theorem, with optimality shown by examples.
en.help_to_user: Provides key analytic tools for higher-codimension minimal surface theory.

idea_spark.idea: Using area growth to control harmonic function growth could be extended to non-minimal surfaces.
idea_spark.risk: Geometric complexity in higher codimension requires careful handling.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining harmonic functions with area growth inspires other geometric analysis questions.

papers.title: Bridging Brain and Semantics: A Hierarchical Framework for Semantically Enhanced fMRI-to-Video Reconstruction
papers.summary: Reconstructing dynamic visual experiences as videos from functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI) is pivotal for advancing the understanding of neural processes. However, current fMRI-to-video reconstruction methods are hindered by a semantic gap between noisy fMRI signals and the rich content of videos, stemming from a reliance on incomplete semantic embeddings that neither capture video-specific cues (e.g., actions) nor integrate prior knowledge. To this end, we draw inspiration from the dual-pathway processing mechanism in human brain and introduce CineNeuron, a novel hierarchical framework for semantically enhanced video reconstruction from fMRI signals with two synergistic stages. First, a bottom-up semantic enrichment stage maps fMRI signals to a rich embedding space that comprehensively captures textual semantics, image contents, action concepts, and object categories. Second, a top-down memory integration stage utilizes the proposed Mixture-of-Memories method to dynamically select relevant "memories" from previously seen data and fuse them with the fMRI embedding to refine the video reconstruction. Extensive experimental results on two fMRI-to-video benchmarks demonstrate that CineNeuron surpasses state-of-the-art methods across various metrics.

ai.tldr: 提出CineNeuron层次框架，通过双阶段（自底向上语义增强+自顶向下记忆融合）提升fMRI到视频的重建质量。
ai.motivation: 现有fMRI到视频重建方法因语义嵌入不完整（缺乏动作等视频特有线索且未整合先验知识）而受限于语义鸿沟。
ai.method: 借鉴人脑双通路处理机制，设计CineNeuron：第一阶段自底向上将fMRI映射到包含文本、图像、动作、物体类别的丰富语义空间；第二阶段自顶向下通过Mixture-of-Memories动态选择相关记忆并融合以细化重建。
ai.result: 在两个基准上超越现有方法，各项指标均达最优。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何利用层次化语义和记忆机制提升脑信号解码视觉体验的精度。

idea_spark.idea: 将Mixture-of-Memories思想用于多模态数据融合，例如结合文本、音频、视频先验增强解码。
idea_spark.risk: 记忆选择可能引入偏差，需验证泛化性；计算开销随记忆库增大而增加。
idea_spark.inspiration: 人脑双通路机制启发层次化设计，可探索更多认知神经科学原理（如注意力、预测编码）用于模型架构。

en.tldr: Proposes CineNeuron, a hierarchical framework with bottom-up semantic enrichment and top-down memory integration to improve fMRI-to-video reconstruction.
en.motivation: Current fMRI-to-video methods suffer from a semantic gap due to incomplete semantic embeddings that lack video-specific cues (e.g., actions) and prior knowledge integration.
en.method: Inspired by the dual-pathway processing in the human brain, CineNeuron first maps fMRI signals to a rich embedding space covering text, image, action, and object categories (bottom-up), then uses Mixture-of-Memories to dynamically select and fuse relevant memories for refined reconstruction (top-down).
en.result: Outperforms state-of-the-art methods on two benchmarks across multiple metrics.
en.help_to_user: Provides insights into leveraging hierarchical semantics and memory mechanisms for accurate brain decoding of visual experiences.

idea_spark.idea: Apply Mixture-of-Memories to multimodal data fusion, e.g., combining text, audio, and video priors for enhanced decoding.
idea_spark.risk: Memory selection may introduce bias; generalization needs verification; computational cost grows with memory size.
idea_spark.inspiration: The dual-pathway mechanism inspires hierarchical design; more cognitive neuroscience principles (e.g., attention, predictive coding) could be explored for model architecture.

papers.title: 3D Skew-Normal Splatting
papers.summary: 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) has emerged as a leading representation for real-time novel view synthesis and been widely adopted in various downstream applications. The core strength of 3DGS lies in its efficient kernel-based scene representation, where Gaussian primitives provide favorable mathematical and computational properties. However, under a finite primitive budget, the symmetric shape of each primitive directly affects representation compactness, especially near asymmetric structures such as object boundaries and one-sided surfaces. Recent works have explored more complex kernel distributions, yet they either remain within the elliptical family or rely on hard truncation, which limits continuous shape control and introduces distributional discontinuities. In this paper, we propose Skew-Normal Splatting (SNS), which adopts the Azzalini Skew-Normal distribution as the fundamental primitive. By introducing a learnable and bounded skewness parameter, SNS can continuously interpolate between symmetric Gaussians and Half-Gaussian-like shapes, enabling flexible modeling of both sharp boundaries and interior regions. Moremover, SNS preserves analytical tractability under affine transformations and marginalization. This property allows seamless integration into existing Gaussian Splatting rasterization pipelines.Furthermore, to address the strong coupling between scale, rotation, and skewness parameters, we introduce a decoupled parameterization and a block-wise optimization strategy to enhance training stability and accuracy. Extensive experiments on standard novel-view synthesis benchmarks show that SNS consistently improves reconstruction quality over Gaussian and recent non-Gaussian kernels, with clearer benefits on sharp boundaries and thin or one-sided structures.

ai.tldr: 提出3D Skew-Normal Splatting (SNS)，用Azzalini Skew-Normal分布替代高斯分布作为基本基元，通过可学习的偏斜参数实现从对称高斯到半高斯形状的连续插值，提升对非对称结构（如物体边界、单侧表面）的重建质量。
ai.motivation: 3D高斯溅射在有限基元预算下，对称高斯基元难以紧凑表示非对称结构（如边界、单侧表面），现有非高斯方法或限于椭圆族、或依赖硬截断，缺乏连续形状控制且引入分布不连续。
ai.method: 采用Azzalini Skew-Normal分布作为基元，引入可学习有界偏斜参数；设计解耦参数化和分块优化策略，解决尺度、旋转与偏斜参数的强耦合问题；保持仿射变换和边缘化的解析可处理性，无缝集成到现有光栅化管线。
ai.result: 在标准新视角合成基准上，SNS一致优于高斯及近期非高斯核，在尖锐边界、薄结构、单侧表面上提升尤其明显。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过引入偏斜正态分布改进3D场景表示，特别是处理非对称几何细节。

idea_spark.idea: 将偏斜正态分布扩展到动态场景或变形场，利用偏斜参数建模随时间变化的非对称运动。
idea_spark.risk: 偏斜参数可能增加优化难度，需谨慎设计初始化与正则化；在极端偏斜下数值稳定性需验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受Azzalini Skew-Normal分布启发，可探索其他参数化非对称分布（如偏斜t分布）在3D表示中的潜力。

idea_spark.idea: 将偏斜正态分布扩展到动态场景或变形场，利用偏斜参数建模随时间变化的非对称运动。

en.tldr: Proposes 3D Skew-Normal Splatting (SNS), replacing Gaussian primitives with the Azzalini Skew-Normal distribution. A learnable bounded skewness parameter enables continuous interpolation from symmetric Gaussian to half-Gaussian shapes, improving reconstruction of asymmetric structures like sharp boundaries and one-sided surfaces.
en.motivation: Under a finite primitive budget, symmetric Gaussian primitives in 3DGS struggle to compactly represent asymmetric structures (e.g., boundaries, one-sided surfaces). Existing non-Gaussian methods either stay within the elliptical family or rely on hard truncation, limiting continuous shape control and introducing discontinuities.
en.method: Adopts the Azzalini Skew-Normal distribution as the primitive with a learnable bounded skewness parameter. Introduces a decoupled parameterization and block-wise optimization to handle strong coupling between scale, rotation, and skewness. Preserves analytical tractability under affine transformations and marginalization for seamless integration into existing rasterization pipelines.
en.result: On standard novel view synthesis benchmarks, SNS consistently outperforms Gaussian and recent non-Gaussian kernels, with clear benefits on sharp boundaries, thin structures, and one-sided surfaces.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how skew-normal distributions can improve 3D scene representation, especially for asymmetric geometric details.

idea_spark.idea: Extend skew-normal splatting to dynamic scenes or deformation fields, using skewness to model time-varying asymmetric motion.
idea_spark.risk: Skewness parameters may increase optimization difficulty; careful initialization and regularization are needed. Numerical stability under extreme skewness requires verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the Azzalini Skew-Normal distribution, explore other parameterized asymmetric distributions (e.g., skew-t) for 3D representation.

papers.title: Know When To Fold 'Em: Token-Efficient LLM Synthetic Data Generation via Multi-Stage In-Flight Rejection
papers.summary: While synthetic data generation with large language models (LLMs) is widely used in post-training pipelines, existing approaches typically generate full outputs before applying quality filters, leading to substantial token waste on samples that are ultimately discarded. To address this, we propose Multi-Stage In-Flight Rejection (MSIFR), a lightweight, training-free framework that detects and terminates low-quality generation trajectories at intermediate checkpoints before they reach full completion. MSIFR decomposes the generation process into sequential stages and applies fast rule-based validators to identify arithmetic inconsistencies, hallucination patterns, and formatting violations, enabling early rejection of faulty samples. We formalize in-flight rejection as a sequential decision process and show that any non-trivial discard policy reduces expected token consumption, with stage-wise savings increasing when rejection occurs earlier in the generation pipeline. We further demonstrate that conditional utility estimates form a martingale, ensuring that early, in-flight rejection does not bias the expected utility of retained samples. Across five instruction-tuned models and seven reasoning benchmarks, MSIFR reduces token consumption by 11%-77% as a standalone method, and up to 78.2% when combined with early-exit methods, while preserving or improving evaluation accuracy. These results confirm that MSIFR provides a practical mechanism for improving the efficiency of LLM-based synthetic data generation without additional training or architectural changes.

ai.tldr: 提出多阶段飞行拒绝（MSIFR）框架，在生成过程中早期检测并终止低质量样本，减少令牌浪费。
ai.motivation: 现有合成数据生成方法先生成完整输出再过滤，导致大量令牌浪费在最终被丢弃的样本上。
ai.method: 将生成过程分解为多个阶段，在每个阶段使用快速规则验证器（如算术一致性、幻觉模式、格式违规）进行早期拒绝，并形式化为序列决策过程。
ai.result: 在五个指令微调模型和七个推理基准上，令牌消耗降低11%-77%，结合早期退出方法最高达78.2%，且保持或提升评估准确率。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户高效生成合成数据，减少计算成本，同时保证数据质量。

idea_spark.idea: 将早期拒绝与动态资源分配结合，根据生成阶段调整验证器复杂度。
idea_spark.risk: 规则验证器可能过于简单，误判高质量样本；需要设计更鲁棒的验证策略。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受强化学习中早期终止策略启发，将生成过程视为马尔可夫决策过程。

en.tldr: Proposes Multi-Stage In-Flight Rejection (MSIFR), a training-free framework that detects and terminates low-quality generation trajectories early, reducing token waste in LLM synthetic data generation.
en.motivation: Existing methods generate full outputs before filtering, wasting tokens on samples that are ultimately discarded.
en.method: Decomposes generation into sequential stages with fast rule-based validators (arithmetic consistency, hallucination patterns, formatting violations) for early rejection, formalized as a sequential decision process.
en.result: Reduces token consumption by 11%-77% standalone, up to 78.2% with early-exit methods, across five models and seven benchmarks, while preserving or improving accuracy.
en.help_to_user: Helps users generate synthetic data more efficiently, lowering computational costs without sacrificing quality.

idea_spark.idea: Combine early rejection with dynamic resource allocation, adjusting validator complexity based on generation stage.
idea_spark.risk: Rule-based validators may misclassify high-quality samples; more robust validation strategies are needed.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by early termination strategies in reinforcement learning, treating generation as a Markov decision process.

papers.title: VGGT-Edit: Feed-forward Native 3D Scene Editing with Residual Field Prediction
papers.summary: High-quality 3D scene reconstruction has recently advanced toward generalizable feed-forward architectures, enabling the generation of complex environments in a single forward pass. However, despite their strong performance in static scene perception, these models remain limited in responding to dynamic human instructions, which restricts their use in interactive applications. Existing editing methods typically rely on a 2D-lifting strategy, where individual views are edited independently and then lifted back into 3D space. This indirect pipeline often leads to blurry textures and inconsistent geometry, as 2D editors lack the spatial awareness required to preserve structure across viewpoints. To address these limitations, we propose VGGT-Edit, a feed-forward framework for text-conditioned native 3D scene editing. VGGT-Edit introduces depth-synchronized text injection to align semantic guidance with the backbone's spatial poses, ensuring stable instruction grounding. This semantic signal is then processed by a residual transformation head, which directly predicts 3D geometric displacements to deform the scene while preserving background stability. To ensure high-fidelity results, we supervise the framework with a multi-term objective function that enforces geometric accuracy and cross-view consistency. We also construct the DeltaScene Dataset, a large-scale dataset generated through an automated pipeline with 3D agreement filtering to ensure ground-truth quality. Experiments show that VGGT-Edit substantially outperforms 2D-lifting baselines, producing sharper object details, stronger multi-view consistency, and near-instant inference speed.

ai.tldr: 提出VGGT-Edit，一种前馈式原生3D场景编辑框架，通过残差场预测实现文本驱动的几何变形，无需2D提升，支持多视角一致且高质量的实时编辑。
ai.motivation: 现有3D场景编辑依赖2D提升策略，导致纹理模糊和几何不一致，且前馈重建模型难以响应动态指令。
ai.method: 引入深度同步文本注入对齐语义与空间姿态，残差变换头直接预测3D几何位移，多目标损失函数保证几何精度和跨视角一致性，并构建DeltaScene数据集。
ai.result: 在几何细节、多视角一致性和推理速度上显著优于2D提升基线，支持近实时编辑。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户快速理解一种无需逐视图编辑、直接操作3D几何的文本驱动场景编辑方法。

idea_spark.idea: 将残差场预测与多模态对齐结合，实现更可控的3D编辑，例如结合语音或手势指令。
idea_spark.risk: 对复杂场景中背景稳定性要求高，数据集质量依赖自动过滤，可能引入偏差。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受前馈重建模型和文本-3D对齐进展启发，直接预测几何位移而非2D提升。

zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户快速理解一种无需逐视图编辑、直接操作3D几何的文本驱动场景编辑方法。

idea_spark.risk: 对复杂场景中背景稳定性要求高，数据集质量依赖自动过滤，可能引入偏差。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受前馈重建模型和文本-3D对齐进展启发，直接预测几何位移而非2D提升。

en.tldr: Proposes VGGT-Edit, a feed-forward native 3D scene editing framework that uses residual field prediction for text-driven geometric deformation, avoiding 2D lifting and enabling multi-view consistent, high-quality real-time editing.
en.motivation: Existing 3D editing relies on 2D lifting, causing blurry textures and geometric inconsistency, and feed-forward reconstruction models cannot respond to dynamic instructions.
en.method: Introduces depth-synchronized text injection to align semantics with spatial poses, a residual transformation head to directly predict 3D geometric displacements, a multi-term loss for geometric accuracy and cross-view consistency, and the DeltaScene Dataset.
en.result: Significantly outperforms 2D-lifting baselines in geometric detail, multi-view consistency, and inference speed, enabling near-instant editing.
en.help_to_user: Helps users quickly understand a text-driven scene editing method that directly manipulates 3D geometry without per-view editing.

idea_spark.idea: Combine residual field prediction with multimodal alignment for more controllable 3D editing, e.g., with voice or gesture commands.
idea_spark.risk: High demand for background stability in complex scenes; dataset quality relies on automatic filtering, which may introduce bias.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by advances in feed-forward reconstruction and text-to-3D alignment, directly predicting geometric displacements instead of 2D lifting.

papers.title: AnyBand-Diff: A Unified Remote Sensing Image Generation and Band Repair Framework with Spectral Priors
papers.summary: Existing diffusion models have made significant progress in generating realistic images. However, their direct adaptation to remote sensing imagery often disregards intrinsic physical laws. This oversight frequently leads to spectral distortion and radiometric inconsistency, severely limiting the scientific utility of generated data. To address this issue, this paper introduces AnyBand-Diff, a novel spectral-prior-guided diffusion framework tailored for robust spectral reconstruction. Specifically, we design a Masked Conditional Diffusion backbone integrated with a dual stochastic masking strategy, empowering the model to recover complete spectral information from arbitrary band subsets. Subsequently, to ensure radiometric fidelity, a Physics-Guided Sampling mechanism is proposed, leveraging gradients from a differentiable physical model to explicitly steer the denoising trajectory toward the manifold of physically plausible solutions. Furthermore, a Multi-Scale Physical Loss is formulated to enforce rigorous constraints across pixel, region, and global levels in a joint manner. Extensive experiments confirm the effectiveness of AnyBand-Diff in generating reliable imagery and achieving accurate spectral reconstruction, contributing to the advancement of physics-aware generative methods for Earth observation.

ai.tldr: 提出AnyBand-Diff框架，利用光谱先验和物理约束实现遥感图像生成与波段修复。
ai.motivation: 现有扩散模型直接用于遥感图像时忽略物理规律，导致光谱失真和辐射不一致。
ai.method: 设计掩码条件扩散主干与双随机掩码策略，结合物理引导采样和多尺度物理损失。
ai.result: 实验证明能生成可靠图像并实现精确光谱重建。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户生成物理一致的遥感图像，修复缺失波段。

idea_spark.idea: 将物理模型梯度作为扩散过程约束，确保生成结果符合物理规律。
idea_spark.risk: 物理模型不准确时可能误导生成方向。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用可微物理模型引导生成，提升科学可用性。

idea_spark.inspiration: 利用可微物理模型引导生成，提升科学可用性。

en.tldr: Proposes AnyBand-Diff, a spectral-prior-guided diffusion framework for remote sensing image generation and band repair with physical constraints.
en.motivation: Existing diffusion models applied to remote sensing ignore physical laws, causing spectral distortion and radiometric inconsistency.
en.method: Designs a masked conditional diffusion backbone with dual stochastic masking, physics-guided sampling using gradients from a differentiable physical model, and multi-scale physical loss.
en.result: Experiments confirm reliable image generation and accurate spectral reconstruction.
en.help_to_user: Helps users generate physically consistent remote sensing images and repair missing bands.

idea_spark.idea: Use gradients from a physical model to constrain the diffusion denoising trajectory.
idea_spark.risk: Inaccurate physical models may misguide generation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leverage differentiable physical models to guide generation for scientific utility.

papers.title: XDomainBench: Diagnosing Reasoning Collapse in High-Dimensional Scientific Knowledge Composition
papers.summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed for knowledge synthesis, yet their capacity for compositional generalization in scientific knowledge remains under-characterized. Existing benchmarks primarily focus on single-turn restricted scenarios, failing to capture the capability boundaries exposed by real-world interactive scientific workflows. To address this, we introduce XDomainBench, a diagnostic benchmark for interactive interdisciplinary scientific reasoning. We formalize the composition order and mixture structure to enable systematic stress-testing from single-discipline to inter-disciplinary, comprising 8,598 interactive sessions across 20 domains and 4 task categories, with 8 realistic trajectory patterns covering difficulty and domain-mixture dynamics, simulating real AI4S scenarios. Large-scale evaluation of LLMs reveals a systematic reasoning collapse as composition order increases, stemming from two root causes: (i) direct difficulty increases induced by domain composition, and (ii) indirect interaction-amplified failures where trajectory patterns trigger error accumulation, reasoning breaks, and domain confusion, ultimately leading to session collapse.

ai.tldr: 提出XDomainBench基准，用于诊断大语言模型在高维科学知识组合中的推理崩溃现象。
ai.motivation: 现有基准主要关注单轮受限场景，无法捕捉交互式科学工作流中LLM的组合泛化能力边界。
ai.method: 形式化组合顺序与混合结构，构建包含8598个交互会话、20个领域、4类任务和8种轨迹模式的诊断基准。
ai.result: 大规模评估发现LLM随组合顺序增加出现系统性推理崩溃，原因包括领域组合导致的难度增加和轨迹模式触发的错误累积、推理中断及领域混淆。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究人员系统评估和诊断LLM在跨学科知识组合任务中的推理能力缺陷。

idea_spark.idea: 基于诊断结果，设计自适应提示策略或训练方法，在组合顺序增加时动态注入领域知识或纠正错误累积。
idea_spark.risk: 基准可能无法覆盖所有真实科学工作流的复杂性，且LLM的推理崩溃可能随模型更新而变化。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类专家在跨学科协作中逐步整合知识的启发，模拟了从单领域到多领域的渐进式组合过程。
