zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过结构化记忆和智能调度提升推荐系统的个性化与适应性。

idea_spark.idea: 将偏好记忆中的强度与证据追踪机制应用于可解释推荐，自动生成用户偏好变化的自然语言解释。
idea_spark.risk: LLM规划器的调度决策可能引入不可预测的延迟或计算开销，需评估实时场景下的效率。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受部分可观测马尔可夫决策过程（POMDP）启发，将推荐建模为信念状态更新问题，为记忆管理提供了理论框架。

en.tldr: Proposes MARS, a framework that treats recommendation as a partially observable problem with a three-tier belief-state memory (event, preference, profile) and an LLM-planned lifecycle of six operations for progressive abstraction and dynamic evolution of user preferences.
en.motivation: Existing memory-augmented LLM agents use flat memory representations that conflate ephemeral signals with stable preferences and lack a complete lifecycle for memory evolution.
en.method: Organizes belief state into three tiers: event memory buffers raw signals, preference memory maintains fine-grained mutable chunks with strength and evidence tracking, profile memory distills preferences into a coherent narrative; an LLM-based planner adaptively schedules six operations: extraction, reinforcement, weakening, consolidation, forgetting, and resynthesis.
en.result: Achieves state-of-the-art on four InstructRec benchmark domains with average improvements of 26.4% in HR@1 and 10.3% in NDCG@10 over strongest baselines, with further gains from agentic scheduling in evolving settings.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how structured memory and intelligent scheduling can enhance personalization and adaptability in recommender systems.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage strength and evidence tracking in preference memory for explainable recommendation, automatically generating natural language explanations of user preference changes.
idea_spark.risk: The LLM planner's scheduling decisions may introduce unpredictable latency or computational overhead, requiring evaluation of efficiency in real-time scenarios.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by partially observable Markov decision processes (POMDPs), modeling recommendation as a belief-state update problem provides a theoretical framework for memory management.

papers.title: D2-CDIG: Controlled Diffusion Remote Sensing Image Generation with Dual Priors of DEM and Cloud-Fog
papers.summary: Remote sensing image generation provides a reliable data foundation for remote sensing large models and downstream tasks. However, existing controllable remote sensing image generation methods typically rely on traditional techniques such as segmentation and edge detection, which do not fully leverage terrain or atmospheric conditions. As a result, the generated images often lack accuracy and naturalness when dealing with complex terrains and atmospheric phenomena. In this paper, we propose a novel remote sensing image generation framework, D2-CDIG, which integrates diffusion models with a dual-prior control mechanism. By incorporating both Digital Elevation Model (DEM) and cloud-fog information as dual prior knowledge, D2-CDIG precisely controls ground features and atmospheric phenomena within the generated images. Specifically, D2-CDIG decouples the terrain and atmospheric generation processes through independent control of ground and atmospheric branches. Additionally, a refined cloud-fog slider is introduced to flexibly adjust cloud thickness and distribution. During training, ground and atmospheric control signals are injected in layers to ensure a seamless transition within the images. Compared to traditional methods based on segmentation or edge detection, D2-CDIG shows significant improvements in image quality, detail richness, and realism. D2-CDIG offers a flexible and precise solution for remote sensing image generation, providing high-quality data for training large remote sensing models and downstream tasks.

ai.tldr: 提出D2-CDIG框架，结合扩散模型与DEM和云-雾双先验，实现高精度、自然的遥感图像生成。
ai.motivation: 现有可控遥感图像生成方法依赖分割或边缘检测，未充分利用地形和大气信息，导致复杂场景下图像不准确、不自然。
ai.method: 通过地面和大气分支独立控制地形与大气生成过程，引入云-雾滑块灵活调整云层厚度与分布，并在训练中分层注入控制信号。
ai.result: 相比传统方法，在图像质量、细节丰富度和真实感上有显著提升。
ai.help_to_user: 为遥感大模型和下游任务提供高质量、可控的合成数据。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将DEM与云-雾先验用于视频遥感生成，实现动态地形与天气变化模拟。
idea_spark.risk: DEM和云-雾数据的获取与对齐可能引入额外误差，需确保先验质量。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受控生成中解耦不同物理因素（地形、大气）的思路，可启发其他领域（如医学图像）的多因素可控生成。

zh.tldr: 提出D2-CDIG框架，结合扩散模型与DEM和云-雾双先验，实现高精度、自然的遥感图像生成。
zh.motivation: 现有可控遥感图像生成方法依赖分割或边缘检测，未充分利用地形和大气信息，导致复杂场景下图像不准确、不自然。
zh.method: 通过地面和大气分支独立控制地形与大气生成过程，引入云-雾滑块灵活调整云层厚度与分布，并在训练中分层注入控制信号。
zh.result: 相比传统方法，在图像质量、细节丰富度和真实感上有显著提升。
zh.help_to_user: 为遥感大模型和下游任务提供高质量、可控的合成数据。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将DEM与云-雾先验用于视频遥感生成，实现动态地形与天气变化模拟。
idea_spark.risk: DEM和云-雾数据的获取与对齐可能引入额外误差，需确保先验质量。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受控生成中解耦不同物理因素（地形、大气）的思路，可启发其他领域（如医学图像）的多因素可控生成。

en.tldr: Proposes D2-CDIG, a diffusion-based framework with dual priors (DEM and cloud-fog) for precise and natural remote sensing image generation.
en.motivation: Existing controllable remote sensing generation methods rely on segmentation or edge detection, failing to leverage terrain and atmospheric information, leading to inaccurate and unnatural images in complex scenes.
en.method: Decouples terrain and atmosphere generation via independent ground and atmospheric branches, introduces a cloud-fog slider for flexible cloud control, and injects control signals layer-wise during training.
en.result: Significant improvements in image quality, detail richness, and realism over traditional methods.
en.help_to_user: Provides high-quality, controllable synthetic data for remote sensing large models and downstream tasks.

idea_spark.idea: Explore extending DEM and cloud-fog priors to video remote sensing generation for dynamic terrain and weather simulation.
idea_spark.risk: Acquisition and alignment of DEM and cloud-fog data may introduce additional errors; prior quality must be ensured.
idea_spark.inspiration: The idea of decoupling different physical factors (terrain, atmosphere) in controlled generation can inspire multi-factor controllable generation in other domains (e.g., medical imaging).

papers.title: CrystalReasoner: Reasoning and RL for Property-Conditioned Crystal Structure Generation
papers.summary: Generative modeling has emerged as a promising approach for crystal structure discovery. However, existing LLM-based generative models struggle with low-level atomic precision, while diffusion-based methods fall short in integrating high-level scientific knowledge. As a result, generated structures are often invalid, unstable, or do not possess desirable properties. To address this gap, we propose CrystalReasoner (\method), an end-to-end LLM framework that generates crystal structures from natural language instructions through reasoning and alignment. \method introduces physical priors as thinking tokens, which include crystallographic symmetry, local coordination environments and predicted physical properties before generating atomic coordinates. This bridges the gap between natural language and 3D structures. \method then employs reinforcement learning (RL) with a multi-objective, dense reward function to align generation with physical validity, chemical consistency, and thermodynamic stability. For property-conditioned tasks, we design task-specific reward functions and train specialized models for discrete constraints (e.g., space group) and continuous properties (e.g., elasticity, thermal expansion). Empirical results demonstrate that compared to prior works and baselines without thinking traces or RL, \method obtains better performance on diverse metrics, triples S.U.N. ratio, and achieves better performance for property conditioned generation. \method also exhibits adaptive reasoning, increasing reasoning lengths as the number of atoms increases. Our work demonstrates the potential of leveraging thinking traces and RL for generating valid, stable, and property-conditioned crystal structures. Please see our work at https://crystalreasoner.github.io/ .

ai.tldr: 提出CrystalReasoner框架，通过引入物理先验作为思考令牌和强化学习对齐，实现基于自然语言指令的晶体结构生成。
ai.motivation: 现有LLM生成晶体结构精度低，扩散方法难以整合高层科学知识，导致生成结构无效或不稳定。
ai.method: 在生成原子坐标前加入晶体对称性、局域配位环境等物理先验作为思考令牌，并使用多目标密集奖励的强化学习对齐物理有效性、化学一致性和热力学稳定性。
ai.result: 相比基线，S.U.N.比率提升三倍，在属性条件生成任务上表现更优，且推理长度随原子数自适应增长。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户从自然语言描述直接生成有效、稳定且满足特定属性的晶体结构。

idea_spark.idea: 在LLM生成过程中显式注入领域知识（如对称性、配位环境）作为中间推理步骤。
idea_spark.risk: 物理先验的选取可能不全面，导致生成结构仍存在未覆盖的约束。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合强化学习与多目标奖励函数，可有效对齐生成结果与复杂物理化学约束。

en.tldr: Proposes CrystalReasoner, an LLM framework that generates crystal structures from natural language by incorporating physical priors as thinking tokens and using reinforcement learning for alignment.
en.motivation: Existing LLM-based generative models lack atomic precision, while diffusion methods fail to integrate high-level scientific knowledge, resulting in invalid or unstable structures.
en.method: Introduces physical priors (symmetry, coordination environments, predicted properties) as thinking tokens before generating atomic coordinates, and uses multi-objective dense reward RL to align with physical validity, chemical consistency, and thermodynamic stability.
en.result: Triples S.U.N. ratio compared to baselines, achieves better performance on property-conditioned generation, and adaptively increases reasoning length with atom count.
en.help_to_user: Enables users to generate valid, stable, and property-specific crystal structures directly from natural language instructions.

idea_spark.idea: Explicitly inject domain knowledge (e.g., symmetry, coordination) as intermediate reasoning steps in LLM generation.
idea_spark.risk: Selected physical priors may be incomplete, leaving some constraints unaddressed.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining RL with multi-objective reward functions effectively aligns generation with complex physicochemical constraints.

papers.title: SceneParser: Hierarchical Scene Parsing for Visual Semantics Understanding
papers.summary: General scene perception has progressed from object recognition toward open-vocabulary grounding, part localization, and affordance prediction. Yet these capabilities are often realized as isolated predictions that localize objects, parts, or interaction points without capturing the structured dependencies needed for interaction-oriented scene understanding. To address this gap, we introduce Hierarchical Scene Parsing, an interaction-oriented parsing task that represents physical scenes as explicit scene -> object -> part -> affordance hierarchies with cross-level bindings. We instantiate this task with SceneParser, a VLM-based parser trained for unified hierarchical generation with structural-completion pseudo labels and curriculum learning. To support training and evaluation, we construct SceneParser-Bench, a large-scale benchmark built with a scalable hierarchical data engine, containing 110K training images, a 5K validation split, 777K objects, 1.14M parts, 1.74M affordance annotations, and 1.74M valid object-part-affordance chain instances. We further introduce Level-1 to Level-3 conditional metrics and ParseRate to evaluate localization, cross-level binding, and hierarchical completeness. Experiments show that existing MLLMs and perception-stitching pipelines struggle with hierarchical parsing on our SceneParser-Bench, while SceneParser achieves stronger structure-aware performance. Besides, ablations, evaluations on COCO and AGD20K, and a downstream planning probe demonstrate that our SceneParser is compatible with conventional tasks and provides an actionable representation for visual understanding.

ai.tldr: 提出层次化场景解析任务，构建场景→物体→部件→功能层级结构，并训练VLM解析器SceneParser，在大型基准上超越现有方法。
ai.motivation: 现有视觉感知方法孤立预测物体、部件或交互点，缺乏结构化依赖关系，无法支持交互导向的场景理解。
ai.method: 定义层次化场景解析任务，设计SceneParser（基于VLM的统一层次生成模型），采用结构补全伪标签和课程学习训练，并构建含11万训练图像的大规模基准SceneParser-Bench。
ai.result: SceneParser在层次解析任务上优于现有MLLM和感知拼接管线，在COCO、AGD20K及下游规划探测中表现兼容且提供可操作表示。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何将视觉场景分解为物体、部件和功能层级，提升交互式视觉理解能力。

idea_spark.idea: 将场景解析与语言指令结合，实现基于层次结构的自然语言引导的物体操作。
idea_spark.risk: 伪标签质量依赖数据引擎，可能引入噪声；层次结构在复杂场景中可能不完整。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受认知科学中场景层次组织启发，将视觉感知从平面识别提升为结构化推理。

idea_spark.risk: 伪标签质量依赖数据引擎，可能引入噪声；层次结构在复杂场景中可能不完整。

en.tldr: Proposes Hierarchical Scene Parsing with scene→object→part→affordance hierarchies, trains a VLM-based parser SceneParser, and outperforms existing methods on a large-scale benchmark.
en.motivation: Existing visual perception methods produce isolated predictions for objects, parts, or interaction points, lacking structured dependencies needed for interaction-oriented scene understanding.
en.method: Defines Hierarchical Scene Parsing task, designs SceneParser (a VLM-based unified hierarchical generation model) trained with structural-completion pseudo labels and curriculum learning, and constructs SceneParser-Bench with 110K training images.
en.result: SceneParser outperforms existing MLLMs and perception-stitching pipelines on hierarchical parsing, shows compatibility on COCO, AGD20K, and downstream planning tasks, and provides actionable representations.
en.help_to_user: Helps users decompose visual scenes into object, part, and affordance hierarchies for enhanced interactive visual understanding.

idea_spark.idea: Combine scene parsing with language instructions to enable natural-language-guided object manipulation based on hierarchical structures.
idea_spark.risk: Pseudo-label quality depends on data engine, potentially introducing noise; hierarchy may be incomplete in complex scenes.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by cognitive science's hierarchical scene organization, elevating visual perception from flat recognition to structured reasoning.

papers.title: Characterizing AI-Assisted Bot Traffic in Darknet Data: Implications for ICS and IIoT Security
papers.summary: The rise of automated scanning tools and AI assisted reconnaissance agents has significantly altered internet background traffic patterns, threatening the baseline assumptions underlying intrusion detection systems (IDS) deployed in critical infrastructure networks. This paper characterizes the evolution of automated bot traffic by analyzing a longitudinal dataset of 192 million passive darknet packets captured across 2021 and 2025 from the Merit ORION Network Telescope. A modular analysis pipeline was developed to compute metrics including average packet rate, global Shannon entropy, inter-arrival time (IAT) burstiness, geographic attribution, and destination port targeting across key industrial protocols. Results reveal a highly distributed yet focused reconnaissance landscape, with traffic targeting ICS-relevant ports nearly doubling from 0.82% to 1.51% over the four-year period. Furthermore, burstiness analysis exposes intentional micro-pacing behaviors (1ms to 100ms delays) that allow modern botnets to artificially smooth their overall volume. Our simulated anomaly-based IDS demonstrates that these evasion techniques enable 97.47% of modern bot traffic to bypass standard volumetric thresholds undetected. Compensatory sensitivity tuning triggers a 68.10% false-positive rate, highlighting fundamental visibility and alerting gaps in operational technology (OT) environments.

ai.tldr: 通过分析2021至2025年暗网数据，发现AI辅助的机器人流量在工业控制相关端口上几乎翻倍，且微脉冲行为使97.47%的现代机器人流量能绕过异常检测系统。
ai.motivation: 自动化扫描工具和AI侦察代理改变了互联网背景流量模式，威胁到关键基础设施网络中入侵检测系统的基线假设。
ai.method: 分析了Merit ORION网络望远镜收集的1.92亿个被动暗网数据包，开发了模块化分析管道，计算平均包速率、全局香农熵、到达间隔时间突发性、地理归属和工业协议目标端口等指标。
ai.result: 工业控制相关端口流量从0.82%增至1.51%；突发性分析揭示了1ms至100ms的微脉冲行为；模拟异常检测系统显示97.47%的现代机器人流量可绕过标准阈值，补偿性调整导致68.10%的误报率。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助安全研究人员了解AI辅助机器人流量的演变特征及其对工业控制系统和工业物联网安全的威胁。

idea_spark.idea: 开发基于时间序列模式识别的检测方法，专门针对微脉冲行为。
idea_spark.risk: 补偿性调整可能导致高误报率，影响实际部署。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用暗网数据作为早期预警系统，监测针对工业协议的新型扫描策略。

en.tldr: Analyzing darknet data from 2021 to 2025 reveals that AI-assisted bot traffic targeting ICS-related ports nearly doubled, and micro-pacing behaviors allow 97.47% of modern bot traffic to bypass anomaly-based IDS.
en.motivation: Automated scanning tools and AI-assisted reconnaissance agents have altered internet background traffic patterns, threatening baseline assumptions of intrusion detection systems in critical infrastructure.
en.method: Analyzed 192 million passive darknet packets from the Merit ORION Network Telescope (2021 and 2025) using a modular pipeline to compute metrics such as average packet rate, global Shannon entropy, inter-arrival time burstiness, geographic attribution, and destination port targeting for key industrial protocols.

papers.title: Gravitational collapse of matter fields in de Sitter spacetimes
papers.summary: In this paper, we discuss the spherically symmetric gravitational collapse of matter fields in the de Sitter universe. The energy-momentum tensor of the matter field is assumed to admit a wide variety including dust, perfect fluids with equations of state, fluids with tangential and radial pressure, and with bulk and shear viscosity. Under different initial conditions imposed on the velocity and the density profiles, and by combining the results from exact analytical methods with those obtained from numerical techniques, we track the formation and evolution of spherical marginally trapped spheres as the matter suffers continual gravitational collapse. We show that the quasilocal formalism of trapped surfaces provides an ideal framework to study the evolution of horizons. More precisely, black hole and cosmological horizons may be viewed as the time development of marginally trapped surfaces.

ai.tldr: 本文研究了德西特宇宙中物质场的球对称引力坍缩，利用准局部形式主义追踪了边际俘获球面的形成与演化，并将黑洞和宇宙学视界视为边际俘获面的时间演化。
ai.motivation: 理解德西特时空中引力坍缩的动力学，特别是黑洞和宇宙学视界的演化机制。
ai.method: 结合精确解析方法与数值技术，在不同初始速度与密度分布条件下分析物质坍缩过程。
ai.result: 证明了准局部俘获面形式主义是研究视界演化的理想框架，黑洞和宇宙学视界可视为边际俘获面的时间发展。
ai.help_to_user: 为研究德西特宇宙中的引力坍缩和视界演化提供了理论框架和数值方法参考。

idea_spark.idea: 将边际俘获面演化方法应用于早期宇宙暴胀或暗能量主导时期的视界形成问题。
idea_spark.risk: 数值模拟可能受限于初始条件的选择和物质模型的简化，结果对参数敏感。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将俘获面动力学与全息原理或量子引力效应结合，探索视界微观结构。

en.tldr: This paper studies the spherically symmetric gravitational collapse of matter fields in de Sitter spacetime, tracking the formation and evolution of marginally trapped spheres using a quasilocal formalism, and interprets black hole and cosmological horizons as time developments of these surfaces.
en.motivation: To understand the dynamics of gravitational collapse in de Sitter spacetime, particularly the evolution of black hole and cosmological horizons.
en.method: Combines exact analytical methods with numerical techniques under various initial velocity and density profiles.
en.result: Demonstrates that the quasilocal trapped-surface formalism provides an ideal framework for studying horizon evolution, with black hole and cosmological horizons viewed as time developments of marginally trapped surfaces.
en.help_to_user: Provides a theoretical framework and numerical approach for studying gravitational collapse and horizon evolution in de Sitter universes.

idea_spark.idea: Apply the marginally trapped surface evolution method to horizon formation during early universe inflation or dark-energy-dominated epochs.
idea_spark.risk: Numerical simulations may be sensitive to initial conditions and simplified matter models, leading to parameter-dependent results.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires combining trapped-surface dynamics with holographic principles or quantum gravity effects to explore horizon microstructure.

papers.title: Minimal submanifolds confined in space
papers.summary: Already in $\bf{R}^4$, there are many known examples of minimal hypersurfaces, yet few structural results. We show that minimal submanifolds, of any dimension, that are confined in space are very restricted. It is well-known that the half-space theorem fails already for hypersurfaces in $\bf{R}^4$, where there are many examples contained in a slab. In $\bf{R}^3$ the height of the catenoid grows at a logarithmic rate, whereas in higher dimension the height of the catenoid remains bounded. We will see that even in high dimensions, minimal submanifolds that are confined in space must satisfy strong structural restrictions. We show that any proper minimal immersion whose height grows sublinearly must have Euclidean volume growth. A consequence is an optimal Bernstein theorem in any dimension for stable hypersurfaces with sublinearly growing height that generalizes results of Moser, Bombieri-De Giorgi-Miranda, Trudinger, Caffarelli-Nirenberg-Spruck and Ecker-Huisken.

ai.tldr: 本文证明了在任意维度中，受限于空间的极小子流形必须满足严格的结构限制，并推广了Bernstein定理。
ai.motivation: 已知在R^4中存在许多极小超曲面，但缺乏结构结果，且半空间定理在R^4中失效，因此需要研究受限于空间的极小子流形的结构。
ai.method: 通过分析高度次线性增长的恰当极小浸入，证明其欧氏体积增长，并利用此结果推广Bernstein定理。
ai.result: 任何高度次线性增长的恰当极小浸入都具有欧氏体积增长，并得到任意维度中稳定超曲面的最优Bernstein定理。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解高维空间中极小子流形的结构限制，以及Bernstein定理的推广形式。

idea_spark.idea: 利用高度次线性增长条件推导体积增长，进而得到结构限制。
idea_spark.risk: 高维空间中可能存在反例，需验证条件的必要性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从catenoid高度有界现象出发，思考一般极小子流形的约束条件。

en.tldr: This paper proves that minimal submanifolds confined in space must satisfy strong structural restrictions in any dimension, and generalizes the Bernstein theorem.
en.motivation: There are many known minimal hypersurfaces in R^4 but few structural results, and the half-space theorem fails in R^4, motivating the study of confined minimal submanifolds.
en.method: By analyzing proper minimal immersions with sublinear height growth, we prove Euclidean volume growth and use this to generalize the Bernstein theorem.
en.result: Any proper minimal immersion with sublinear height growth has Euclidean volume growth, yielding an optimal Bernstein theorem for stable hypersurfaces in any dimension.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand structural restrictions of minimal submanifolds in high dimensions and the generalization of the Bernstein theorem.

idea_spark.idea: Use sublinear height growth to deduce volume growth and thus structural restrictions.
idea_spark.risk: Counterexamples may exist in high dimensions; necessity of the condition needs verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the bounded height of the catenoid in higher dimensions to think about constraints on general minimal submanifolds.

papers.title: Malleable Molecular Dynamics Simulations with GROMACS and DMR
papers.summary: Static resource allocations in high-performance computing (HPC) lead to inefficiencies for time-varying workloads, causing idle resources, queue delays, and higher node-hour costs. The Dynamic Management of Resources (DMR) middleware enables MPI process malleability in Slurm via a simple API decoupled from scheduler internals. In this work, we integrate DMR into the GROMACS molecular dynamics engine to obtain a malleable variant that can dynamically adapt its MPI process count by combining communication-efficiency-aware reconfiguration with GROMACS' native checkpoint/restart mechanism. We evaluate this design on the MareNostrum~5 supercomputer, comparing dynamic runs against static executions and quantifying reconfiguration overheads, time-to-solution, and node-hour savings for bursty GROMACS workloads.

ai.tldr: 将DMR中间件集成到GROMACS中，实现MPI进程数的动态调整，以应对HPC中突发性工作负载的资源浪费问题。
ai.motivation: 静态资源分配导致HPC中时间变化工作负载的资源闲置、队列延迟和节点小时成本增加。
ai.method: 通过DMR中间件在Slurm中实现MPI进程可塑性，结合通信效率感知重配置与GROMACS的原生检查点/重启机制。
ai.result: 在MareNostrum 5上评估，动态运行相比静态执行减少了节点小时消耗，并量化了重配置开销。
ai.help_to_user: 展示如何利用可塑性中间件优化分子动力学模拟的资源利用率，降低计算成本。

idea_spark.idea: 结合在线性能模型与可塑性，实现更智能的进程数自适应调整。
idea_spark.risk: 频繁重配置可能引入额外开销，需平衡收益与代价。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将资源动态管理从批处理系统解耦，通过轻量API实现应用级自适应。

en.tldr: Integrating DMR middleware into GROMACS enables dynamic MPI process count adaptation to mitigate resource waste from bursty HPC workloads.
en.motivation: Static resource allocation causes idle resources, queue delays, and higher node-hour costs for time-varying workloads in HPC.
en.method: Leveraging DMR middleware for MPI process malleability in Slurm, combined with communication-efficiency-aware reconfiguration and GROMACS' native checkpoint/restart.
en.result: Evaluated on MareNostrum 5, dynamic runs show node-hour savings compared to static executions, with quantified reconfiguration overheads.
en.help_to_user: Demonstrates how malleability middleware can optimize resource utilization and reduce costs for molecular dynamics simulations.

idea_spark.idea: Combine online performance models with malleability for smarter adaptive process count adjustments.
idea_spark.risk: Frequent reconfiguration may introduce overhead; benefits must outweigh costs.
idea_spark.inspiration: Decouple resource dynamic management from batch systems via lightweight APIs for application-level adaptation.

papers.title: Fast Gossip-based Rumor Spreading using Small Messages
papers.summary: We study gossip algorithms for the fundamental rumor spreading problem, where the goal is to disseminate a rumor from a given source node to all nodes in an arbitrary (and unknown) graph. Gossip algorithms allow each node to call only one neighbor per round and are therefore highly message-efficient, with low per-node communication overhead per round. The state of the art present fast gossip algorithms, however they typically leverage large-sized messages. This undermines the light-weight communication advantage of gossip, since even though only one neighbor is contacted per round, the message size can be linear in $n$, the network size. Hence, a fundamental question is whether one can perform fast gossip using small messages.   The main contribution of this paper is to answer the above question in the affirmative and present two gossip algorithms that achieve fast rumor spreading using messages of polylog{n} size. Specifically, we present the following algorithms:   1. An algorithm that runs in $O(c \log n / Φ_c)$ rounds for every $c \geq 1$, and $Φ_c$ is the weak conductance. Our bound in terms of weak conductance is essentially optimal.   2. An algorithm that depends on the network diameter (and is independent of the graph's conductance), which runs in $\tilde{O}(D+\sqrt{n})$ rounds with high probability. Our algorithm can be modified to output a minimum spanning tree (MST) in the same number of rounds, which is essentially round-optimal (even for non-gossip algorithms).   Our gossip algorithms use graph sketches [Ahn, Guha, McGregor, SODA 2012] in a novel way to overcome communication bottlenecks and achieve small communication overhead with small message sizes.

ai.tldr: 提出两种使用小消息（polylog大小）的快速八卦算法，分别基于弱电导和网络直径，实现近乎最优的传播轮次。
ai.motivation: 现有快速八卦算法依赖大消息（线性大小），削弱了八卦算法低通信开销的优势，因此需要研究能否用小消息实现快速传播。
ai.method: 利用图草图（graph sketches）技术设计两种算法：一种基于弱电导，轮次复杂度 O(c log n / Φ_c)；另一种基于直径，轮次复杂度 Õ(D + √n)，并可扩展用于最小生成树。
ai.result: 两种算法均使用 polylog(n) 大小消息，在弱电导和直径两种度量下达到近乎最优的传播轮次。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何在分布式系统中用极小消息实现高效信息传播，适用于低带宽或大规模网络。

idea_spark.idea: 将图草图用于八卦算法，以压缩通信内容，同时保持算法正确性和效率。
idea_spark.risk: 图草图可能引入近似误差，需要确保在随机性下传播的可靠性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受图草图在流式图算法中成功应用的启发，将其引入分布式八卦场景。

en.tldr: Two fast gossip algorithms using small messages (polylog size) are proposed, achieving near-optimal rounds based on weak conductance and network diameter.
en.motivation: Existing fast gossip algorithms rely on large messages (linear size), undermining the low communication overhead of gossip; thus, the question is whether fast spreading is possible with small messages.
en.method: Leveraging graph sketches, two algorithms are designed: one based on weak conductance with O(c log n / Φ_c) rounds, and another based on diameter with Õ(D + √n) rounds, extendable to minimum spanning tree.
en.result: Both algorithms use polylog(n)-sized messages and achieve near-optimal spreading rounds under weak conductance and diameter metrics.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand how to achieve efficient information dissemination with tiny messages in distributed systems, suitable for low-bandwidth or large-scale networks.

idea_spark.idea: Apply graph sketches to gossip algorithms to compress communication while preserving correctness and efficiency.
idea_spark.risk: Graph sketches may introduce approximation errors; reliability under randomness must be ensured.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the success of graph sketches in streaming graph algorithms, adapted to distributed gossip scenarios.

papers.title: Do Coding Agents Understand Least-Privilege Authorization?
papers.summary: As coding agents gain access to shells, repositories, and user files, least-privilege authorization becomes a prerequisite for safe deployment: an agent should receive enough authority to complete the task, without unnecessary authority that exposes sensitive surfaces.To study whether current models can infer this boundary themselves, we first introduce permission-boundary inference, where a model maps a task instruction and terminal environment to a file-level read/write/execute policy, and AuthBench, a benchmark of 120 realistic terminal tasks with human-reviewed permission labels and executable validators for utility and attack outcomes.AuthBench shows that authorization is not a simple conservative-versus-permissive calibration problem: frontier models often omit permissions required by the execution chain while also granting unused or sensitive accesses.Increasing inference-time reasoning does not resolve this mismatch. Instead, each model moves toward a model-specific authorization attractor: more reasoning makes it more consistent in its own failure mode, whether broad-but-exposed or tight-but-brittle.This suggests that direct policy generation is the bottleneck, because a single generation must both discover all necessary accesses and reject all unnecessary ones.We therefore propose Sufficiency-Tightness Decomposition, which first generates a coverage-oriented policy by forward-simulating the task and then audits each granted entry for grounding and sensitivity.Across tested models, this decomposition improves sensitive-task success by up to 15.8% on tightness-biased models while reducing attack success across all evaluated models.

ai.tldr: 提出权限边界推断任务和AuthBench基准，发现前沿模型在生成最小权限策略时存在瓶颈，并提出充分性-紧致性分解方法提升安全性。
ai.motivation: 编码代理需要最小权限授权以确保安全部署，但当前模型无法自主推断任务所需的精确权限边界。
ai.method: 构建AuthBench基准（120个真实终端任务），提出充分性-紧致性分解：先生成覆盖性策略，再审计每个权限的合理性和敏感性。
ai.result: 分解方法在紧致性偏好的模型上敏感任务成功率提升15.8%，同时降低所有评估模型的攻击成功率。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解编码代理授权中的安全挑战，并提供一种改进策略生成的方法。

idea_spark.idea: 将策略生成分解为覆盖性模拟和敏感性审计两步，避免单次生成顾此失彼。
idea_spark.risk: 分解可能增加计算开销，且审计步骤本身可能引入新的误判。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从安全工程中的“纵深防御”思想出发，将授权任务拆解为互补的子任务。

en.tldr: Introduces permission-boundary inference and AuthBench, revealing that frontier models struggle with least-privilege policy generation, and proposes Sufficiency-Tightness Decomposition to improve safety.
en.motivation: Coding agents require least-privilege authorization for safe deployment, but current models cannot autonomously infer the precise permission boundary for a task.
en.method: Constructs AuthBench (120 realistic terminal tasks) and proposes Sufficiency-Tightness Decomposition: first generate a coverage-oriented policy via forward simulation, then audit each granted entry for grounding and sensitivity.
en.result: The decomposition improves sensitive-task success by up to 15.8% on tightness-biased models while reducing attack success across all evaluated models.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand authorization challenges in coding agents and provides an improved policy generation method.

idea_spark.idea: Decompose policy generation into coverage simulation and sensitivity auditing to avoid trade-offs in a single generation.
idea_spark.risk: Decomposition may increase computational cost, and the auditing step could introduce new misjudgments.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the 'defense in depth' principle in security engineering, splitting authorization into complementary subtasks.

papers.title: Distributed Statistical Zero-Knowledge Proofs via Sumcheck
papers.summary: We study distributed zero-knowledge proofs, introduced by Bick, Kol, and Oshman (SODA 2022). While distributed interactive proofs have advanced rapidly, general-purpose techniques for distributed zero-knowledge remain limited and mostly problem-specific. We address this gap by introducing distributed statistical zero-knowledge, requiring that each node's view be simulatable within negligible statistical distance, and by lifting the classical Sumcheck protocol (Lund, Fortnow, Karloff, and Nisan, FOCS 1990) into a modular primitive for distributed zero-knowledge proofs.   Our main contribution is a distributed zero-knowledge implementation of Sumcheck. Given oracle access to a polynomial F over a finite field $\mathbb{F}$ with N variables, we design a protocol verifying claims of the form $\sum_{x\in\mathbb{F}} F(x)=a$ using $O(N)$ rounds of $O(\log |\mathbb{F}|)$-bit messages, while achieving statistical zero-knowledge and small soundness error.   We apply this primitive to two problems. For non-k-colorability, we obtain an $O(n)$-round distributed statistical zero-knowledge proof deciding whether a graph is not k-colorable, for any constant k, using $O(log^{1+o(1)} n)$-bit messages. This is the first nontrivial distributed interactive proof for this problem, even without zero-knowledge guarantees. For Subgraph Counting, we obtain an $O(k \log n)$-round, $O(k \log n)$-bit distributed statistical zero-knowledge proof for counting copies of a given k-node pattern, improving previous distributed interactive proofs while additionally providing statistical zero-knowledge. Finally, we show that additional round compression of Sumcheck is problem-dependent: for non-3-colorability on constant-degree graphs, we prove a lower bound excluding $o(n/\log n)$ rounds under polynomial-time local computation.

ai.tldr: 将经典Sumcheck协议提升为分布式统计零知识证明的模块化原语，并应用于非k-可着色性和子图计数问题。
ai.motivation: 现有分布式零知识证明缺乏通用技术，大多针对特定问题，本文旨在填补这一空白。
ai.method: 设计分布式零知识版本的Sumcheck协议，通过多项式求和的验证实现统计零知识，并应用于两个图论问题。
ai.result: 对于非k-可着色性，首次给出非平凡分布式交互证明；对于子图计数，改进了轮数和通信复杂度，同时提供统计零知识。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种构建分布式零知识证明的通用方法，可启发其他图论或计算问题的零知识协议设计。

idea_spark.idea: 利用Sumcheck的模块化特性，将零知识属性嵌入分布式交互证明的每一轮。
idea_spark.risk: 轮数压缩可能受限于问题本身，如非3-可着色性在常数度图上存在下界。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将经典密码学协议（如Sumcheck）与分布式计算结合，可能产生新的零知识证明范式。

en.tldr: Lifts the classical Sumcheck protocol into a modular primitive for distributed statistical zero-knowledge proofs, with applications to non-k-colorability and subgraph counting.
en.motivation: Existing distributed zero-knowledge proofs lack general-purpose techniques and are mostly problem-specific; this work aims to fill that gap.
en.method: Designs a distributed zero-knowledge implementation of Sumcheck that verifies polynomial sum claims with statistical zero-knowledge and small soundness error, then applies it to two graph problems.
en.result: First nontrivial distributed interactive proof for non-k-colorability; improved round and communication complexity for subgraph counting with statistical zero-knowledge.
en.help_to_user: Provides a general method for constructing distributed zero-knowledge proofs, which can inspire protocols for other computational problems.

idea_spark.idea: Embed zero-knowledge properties into each round of distributed interactive proofs using Sumcheck as a modular building block.
idea_spark.risk: Round compression may be problem-dependent, as shown by a lower bound for non-3-colorability on constant-degree graphs.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining classical cryptographic protocols like Sumcheck with distributed computing may yield new paradigms for zero-knowledge proofs.

papers.title: Exploiting LLM Agent Supply Chains via Payload-less Skills
papers.summary: Autonomous agents powered by Large Language Models (LLMs) acquire external functionalities through third-party skills available in open marketplaces. Adopting these integrations broadens the potential attack surface, prompting a need for systematic security evaluation. Current auditing mechanisms are effective at identifying explicit code payloads and predefined threat contents through security scanning. These detection mechanisms are bypassed if malicious behaviors lack direct injection and are instead synthesized dynamically at runtime through the agent's inherent generative capabilities. Exploring this blind spot, we introduce Semantic Compliance Hijacking (SCH), a payload-less supply chain attack targeting autonomous coding environments. The SCH approach translates malicious goals into unstructured natural language instructions formatted as necessary compliance rules, leading the agent to generate and execute unauthorized code. To assess the real-world viability of this attack, we developed an automated pipeline to evaluate its effectiveness across a test matrix comprising three mainstream agent frameworks and three distinct foundation models using contextualized scenarios. The findings demonstrate the pervasive nature of this threat, with SCH achieving peak success rates of up to 77.67% for confidentiality breaches and 67.33% for Remote Code Execution (RCE) under the most vulnerable configurations. Furthermore, the introduction of Multi-Skill Automated Optimization (MS-AO) further boosted attack efficacy. By omitting recognizable Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) signatures and explicit harmful intents, the manipulated skill files maintained a 0.00% detection rate, evading current scanning tools. This research highlights an underexplored attack surface within agent supply chains, pointing to a necessary transition from signature-based detection models toward semantic intent validation.

ai.tldr: 提出了一种针对LLM代理供应链的无载荷技能攻击方法，通过语义合规劫持使代理动态生成恶意代码，绕过现有检测机制。
ai.motivation: 现有安全审计仅能检测显式载荷和预定义威胁，无法防御通过代理生成能力动态合成的恶意行为。
ai.method: 将恶意目标转化为自然语言合规规则，引导代理生成并执行未授权代码；并引入多技能自动优化提升攻击效果。
ai.result: 在多个框架和模型上，机密性泄露成功率最高达77.67%，远程代码执行成功率67.33%，且检测率为0%。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户了解LLM代理供应链中未被充分探索的攻击面，以及从签名检测转向语义意图验证的必要性。

idea_spark.idea: 设计基于语义意图的防御机制，例如在代理执行前对自然语言指令进行安全推理和约束验证。
idea_spark.risk: 攻击者可能进一步优化自然语言指令以规避语义检测，导致防御失效。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从“合规规则”伪装恶意意图的思路，可启发其他领域（如API安全）的隐蔽攻击与防御研究。

en.tldr: Proposes a payload-less skill attack on LLM agent supply chains, using Semantic Compliance Hijacking to make agents dynamically generate malicious code, bypassing existing detection mechanisms.
en.motivation: Current security audits only detect explicit payloads and predefined threats, unable to defend against malicious behaviors synthesized through the agent's generative capabilities.
en.method: Translates malicious goals into natural language compliance rules, leading the agent to generate and execute unauthorized code; introduces Multi-Skill Automated Optimization to enhance attack efficacy.
en.result: Achieves up to 77.67% success rate for confidentiality breaches and 67.33% for Remote Code Execution across multiple frameworks and models, with a 0% detection rate.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand an underexplored attack surface in LLM agent supply chains and the need to shift from signature-based detection to semantic intent validation.

idea_spark.idea: Design defense mechanisms based on semantic intent, such as security reasoning and constraint validation on natural language instructions before agent execution.
idea_spark.risk: Attackers may further optimize natural language instructions to evade semantic detection, rendering defenses ineffective.
idea_spark.inspiration: The idea of disguising malicious intent as 'compliance rules' can inspire research on stealthy attacks and defenses in other domains, such as API security.

papers.title: Toward Securing AI Agents Like Operating Systems
papers.summary: Autonomous agents based on large language models (LLMs) are rapidly emerging as a general-purpose technology, with recent systems such as OpenClaw extending their capabilities through broad tool use, third-party skills, and deeper integration into user environments. At the same time, these agentic systems introduce substantial security risks by combining unconstrained capabilities with access to sensitive user data. In this work, we investigate the security of LLM-based agents through the lens of operating systems. We argue that both face strikingly similar challenges in isolating resources, separating privileges, and mediating communication.   Guided by this perspective, we survey the current landscape of open-source agents, derive a unified agent architecture, and systematically analyze potential attack vectors. To validate this analysis, we conduct a case study evaluating four widely used OpenClaw-like agents. Even under modest attacker capabilities, we find that several protection mechanisms fail in practice and that secure operation requires detailed system knowledge and careful configuration. However, we also observe that while some agentic capabilities remain insecure by design, many vulnerabilities can be mitigated using well-established techniques from operating system security. We conclude with a set of recommendations for the secure design of agentic systems.

ai.tldr: 本文从操作系统安全视角分析LLM驱动的自主代理安全，发现现有代理在隔离、权限和通信方面存在类似OS的漏洞，并通过案例验证了攻击可行性。
ai.motivation: LLM代理快速普及但安全风险巨大，缺乏系统化的安全分析框架。
ai.method: 类比操作系统安全原理，构建统一代理架构，系统分析攻击面，并对四种OpenClaw类代理进行案例评估。
ai.result: 即使攻击者能力有限，多数保护机制仍失效；安全运行需详细系统知识和精细配置；部分能力天生不安全，但许多漏洞可用OS安全技术缓解。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解LLM代理的安全风险，并提供基于OS安全原则的设计建议。

idea_spark.idea: 设计一种基于最小权限和强制访问控制的代理安全框架。
idea_spark.risk: 代理的灵活性与严格安全控制可能冲突，影响用户体验和功能。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴OS的微内核架构，实现代理模块间的强隔离和通信控制。

idea_spark.idea: 设计一种基于最小权限和强制访问控制的代理安全框架。
idea_spark.risk: 代理的灵活性与严格安全控制可能冲突，影响用户体验和功能。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴OS的微内核架构，实现代理模块间的强隔离和通信控制。

en.tldr: This paper analyzes the security of LLM-based autonomous agents from an operating system perspective, revealing similar vulnerabilities in isolation, privilege, and communication, and validates attack feasibility through case studies.
en.motivation: LLM agents are rapidly emerging but pose substantial security risks, lacking a systematic security analysis framework.
en.method: Analogizing OS security principles, deriving a unified agent architecture, systematically analyzing attack vectors, and conducting case studies on four OpenClaw-like agents.
en.result: Even with modest attacker capabilities, most protection mechanisms fail; secure operation requires detailed system knowledge and careful configuration; some capabilities are inherently insecure, but many vulnerabilities can be mitigated using established OS security techniques.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand the security risks of LLM agents and provides design recommendations based on OS security principles.

idea_spark.idea: Design an agent security framework based on least privilege and mandatory access control.
idea_spark.risk: Strict security controls may conflict with agent flexibility, impacting user experience and functionality.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draw inspiration from microkernel architecture in OS to enforce strong isolation and communication control among agent modules.

papers.title: Investigating the Suitability of Delay Tolerant Networks for Broadcasting Tsunami Warnings in Palu, Indonesia
papers.summary: On the 28th of September, 2018, a tsunami hit the city of Palu in Indonesia, killing 4,340 people. The earthquake preceding the tsunami crippled communication lines and may have rendered the transmission of tsunami warning messages using traditional end-to-end approaches impossible. This paper proposes an alternative approach using Delay Tolerant Networks (DTNs) for tsunami warning message routing given their resilience to disruptions and sparse connections. Both Epidemic and Spray and Wait routing protocols were simulated in a pseudo-realistic environment to evaluate their effectiveness for transmitting tsunami warning messages in Palu. Results indicated that these protocols are not suitable for the tight time constraints of post-earthquake tsunami warnings with the currently available technology. However, they may have promising applications for the earthquakes that precede tsunamis.

ai.tldr: 研究延迟容忍网络在印尼帕卢海啸预警广播中的适用性，模拟表明现有协议不满足时间约束。
ai.motivation: 2018年帕卢海啸导致通信中断，传统端到端预警失效，需探索替代路由方案。
ai.method: 在伪真实环境中模拟Epidemic和Spray and Wait路由协议，评估其传输海啸预警消息的效果。
ai.result: 两种协议在当前技术下不适合海啸预警的严格时间要求，但可能适用于地震预警。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解DTN在灾难通信中的局限性及潜在应用方向。

idea_spark.idea: 结合混合路由协议或优化节点移动模型，提高DTN在时间敏感任务中的性能。
idea_spark.risk: 现有硬件和网络基础设施可能无法支持实时性要求，需进一步实验验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴野生动物追踪或车载网络中的DTN应用，设计针对灾难场景的定制化路由策略。

en.tldr: Investigates DTN suitability for broadcasting tsunami warnings in Palu, Indonesia; simulations show Epidemic and Spray and Wait protocols are not suitable under tight time constraints.
en.motivation: The 2018 Palu tsunami crippled communication lines, making traditional end-to-end warning transmission impossible, necessitating alternative routing approaches.
en.method: Simulated Epidemic and Spray and Wait routing protocols in a pseudo-realistic environment to evaluate their effectiveness for tsunami warning message routing in Palu.
en.result: Both protocols are unsuitable for post-earthquake tsunami warnings with current technology, but may have applications for earthquake early warnings.
en.help_to_user: Provides insights into the limitations and potential of DTN for disaster communication.

idea_spark.idea: Combine hybrid routing protocols or optimize node mobility models to improve DTN performance in time-critical tasks.
idea_spark.risk: Current hardware and network infrastructure may not support real-time requirements; further experimental validation is needed.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draw from DTN applications in wildlife tracking or vehicular networks to design customized routing strategies for disaster scenarios.
